escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora demands proof, numbers, and visible procedure before consent

thresholds.at_2_instances: Riven trims ideology and starts front-loading measurable risk in briefings.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Riven's scenes with Nora become openly contractual; hidden contingencies trigger sharper confrontation beats.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: By late sessions, Riven cannot plausibly ask for blind trust at all; every invasive act is framed as debt, witness, or breach.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora spends body or port integrity for truth and speed

thresholds.at_2_instances: Jace's warnings become more concrete and protective; symptom narration appears sooner.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Riven treats Nora as necessary volunteer or dangerous martyr, and Mirror access escalates through cleaner trace channels.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Late-session prose leans hard into failure window, blackout risk, and irreversible cost; allies stop pretending recovery is likely.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora frames alliance in utility rather than trust

thresholds.at_2_instances: Jace keeps more emotional distance and shares less unprompted vulnerability.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Bond scenes grow brittle; rescue and comfort beats require explicit reversal choices to thaw.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: By Sessions 4-5, Jace speaks from settled hurt restraint, and even loyalty reads like chosen labor rather than mutual refuge.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora enforces rules, vows, and consent structures around dangerous cooperation

thresholds.at_2_instances: Jace trusts Nora's spoken boundaries and follows her field rules more readily.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Promises_made becomes a major relationship engine; breaking one lands harder than a generic tactical error.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Late scenes evaluate Nora through vow-keeping logic; fulfilled rules create rare earned warmth, broken rules create near-irreversible trust fractures.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora weaponizes grief objects or uses the lens aggressively against the system

thresholds.at_2_instances: Mirror responses shift from seduction toward architecture and pressure.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Jace increasingly reads mission choices as costly object-spending, and Riven offers harsher breach logic sooner.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story's symbolic texture around the lens hardens into blade/key language, making the final relic state feel scarred rather than tender.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path through measured spacing, clipped tactical exchanges, and Jace offering facts before trust whenever the bond is tested.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Trust must be earned under pressure, not granted because the tether says so.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to deny the tether is real.
Do not let freeform input fully abandon Jace on the platform.
Do not introduce speeches of wounded intimacy from Jace.
Do not bypass the forced cooperation spine.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can keep her distance, test him, or demand something concrete first.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Footsteps and active reflections collapse the time available, so the world only permits a brief proof exchange before flight becomes mandatory.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with sudden motion, broken-route consequences, and Jace reacting to Nora as someone who converts panic into action before permission arrives.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Movement is safer than uncertainty, even if it burns the clean route.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to overpower Jace into hostility or separation.
Do not invent combat victory over Mirror Agents here.
Do not remove the tether surge cost.
Do not turn the escape into stylish action banter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can shove, drag, cut through the wrong door, or choose motion over explanation.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The service gate and pursuit geometry absorb the force of that choice; Jace comes because the route behind is already closing.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having Jace obey quickly, routes stay orderly for a beat, and then the world itself invalidate the clean plan through visible reflection threat.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Control is a real value even when the world refuses to reward it for long.
hard_limits: Do not let procedure make the route safe all the way out.
Do not remove reflective surfaces as threat vectors.
Do not make Jace submissive beyond his established practicality.
Do not allow full trust or intimacy at first contact.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can establish order, assign pace, and insist he follow her line.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The visible route fails because the glass becomes active threat, forcing the same alliance into a harsher corridor.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path through named components, visible readouts, and Riven treating access to the lens and Nora's body as something that must be spoken aloud.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Grief can be protected by turning it into procedure instead of surrender.
hard_limits: Do not allow the lens to stay completely outside the decode workflow.
Do not make Riven suddenly warm or transparent.
Do not erase bodily risk through procedure.
Do not introduce legalistic exposition detached from scene pressure.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can set terms, demand visibility, and require named cost before access.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The decode still has to happen, so Riven accepts constraints and rebuilds the room as a contract rather than a theft.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with the lens staying physically close to Nora while the room grows messier around workarounds and incomplete projection.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: One real thing remains under Nora's custody even if the mission has to suffer for it.
hard_limits: Do not allow total refusal to use the lens at all.
Do not let the workaround produce perfect clarity.
Do not shift Jace into overt sentimentality about the object.
Do not stall the story away from the first pass.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can keep the object, limit access, or insist they work from pattern rather than possession.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The rig can only get fragments without full surrender, so the world answers with slower, dirtier, partial decode.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path by treating the lens as mounted hardware, drawing harsher readouts and making Jace read Nora as willing to weaponize even grief.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: If the object can cut the system, spending it is justified.
hard_limits: Do not destroy the lens here.
Do not let aggressive use remove decode cost.
Do not let Riven become gleeful or theatrical.
Do not detach the tech from bodily consequence.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can hand it over hard, push speed, or order the rig to use the crack as a weapon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world cashes that intent as a hard-mount configuration with immediate risk warnings instead of a consequence-free power move.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path through symptom-forward prose, Jace's tightened physical vigilance, and Riven treating Nora's body as already spent into the mission.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Truth now is worth bodily damage now.
hard_limits: Do not allow the decode to be painless.
Do not grant perfect clarity without port degradation.
Do not let Jace stop the pass outright.
Do not turn Nora's choice into melodramatic martyr speech.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can rush the chair, demand the pass, or refuse delay.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Once contact is made, the machine answers with immediate bodily cost and only partial truth, preserving the story's hardware rule.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path with monitors, thresholds, and witness language whenever Nora faces invasive risk or hidden math later.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Consent means understanding the numbers, not just hearing necessity.
hard_limits: Do not let scrutiny cancel the first decode entirely.
Do not reveal the full future map here.
Do not make Riven evasive beyond her canon; she must provide some raw truth.
Do not break the session's need for the pass to proceed.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can interrogate readouts, demand fallback plans, and force the machine to speak plainly first.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Urgency holds, but the room can be slowed into witness long enough for proof before the inevitable pass.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by repeatedly showing visible safeguards, exposed kill switches, and Riven having to operate inside accountability she did not choose.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Necessity does not erase the right to refuse violation repeated in cleaner language.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to avoid the decode forever.
Do not remove the countdown cost of delay.
Do not transform Riven into an apologetic caretaker.
Do not let safeguards eliminate bodily danger.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can refuse blind insertion, require visible cutoff, and make the room answer for access to her body.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world grants structural safeguards but charges for them in lost time and rising countdown pressure.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Carry this path forward by having later alliance scenes remember the spoken rules and judge everyone by whether they honor them under stress.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Staying is possible if the bond is framed by named limits rather than faith.
hard_limits: Do not let Nora leave the alliance before Session 3 begins.
Do not resolve the next pass inside this session.
Do not make Riven emotionally comforting in her agreement.
Do not let the rules become magic protection.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can stay on conditions, set terms aloud, and make both of them hear the perimeter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The session ends once those terms are accepted because the next test of them belongs to the next decode room, not this one.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path through colder spacing, route-first dialogue, and Jace's continued cooperation carrying a measurable emotional withdrawal.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Utility can hold an alliance together even when trust cannot.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to sever from Jace and Riven completely.
Do not erase the tether's emotional charge altogether.
Do not make Jace lash out theatrically.
Do not let mission-only framing skip the next pass.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can stay for the map, refuse belief language, and keep the alliance functional rather than intimate.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world accepts that colder contract because the route still needs all three of you, but it marks the cost in Jace's measured withdrawal.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path through momentum, reduced preamble, and allies acting as though Nora's commitment has already burned away one layer of hesitation.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Commitment itself can become propulsion, even when retreat would be wiser.
hard_limits: Do not let commitment resolve the danger.
Do not make Jace instantly intimate or unguarded beyond canon restraint.
Do not remove bodily cost from the next pass.
Do not let Riven reveal every secret immediately.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Nora can commit fast, close the retreat route, and choose forward motion over protected distance.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The session cuts on that vow because the consequence is not a speech but the next room opening already in motion.

session_close_design.resolution_prose:
The rig unlatches with a wet click at the base of your skull. Wire marks. Port heat. Then the cold after. Riven’s hands are already on the clamps, fast and exact, while the screen above you holds the verdict in hard white text: DECODE COMPLETE: 87%.

Not a ghost. Not static. A voice.

Eli.

You heard him. Thin. Broken apart. Real enough to bruise. Three words dragged through dead channels and signal rot, but his voice all the same. The cracked lens is still under your coat, pressed flat to your sternum, warm now from your body instead of his. Jace stands just beyond the rig, hollow-eyed, still here. Riven wipes the blood at her nose with the back of her wrist and does not dress it up.

Another readout blooms. NEURAL PORT INTEGRITY: 3%.
RECOVERY WINDOW: LIMITED.
RESET COUNTDOWN: ACTIVE.

The room smells like hot dust and antiseptic.

You came for the Eli thread.
You found it.

Not all of him. Enough.

Enough to make the next question real.
session_close_design.hook_transition:
For one breath, nobody asks anything. The tone in your right ear keeps singing anyway.

Then Riven angles the monitor toward you. New waveform. New fracture in the stream. Jace looks at you, not the screen. Waiting for your call.

session_end_choice.choice_question: What do you do with the fragment of Eli you just pulled out of the decode?

option_a.text: Run one more pass now, before the port fails.
option_a.next_session_opens: The next session opens with Nora back in the rig, choosing urgency over safety as the decode pushes past recovery limits.

option_b.text: Stop the decode and question Jace about what he recognized in Eli’s voice.
option_b.next_session_opens: The next session opens in the aftermath chamber, with Nora forcing Jace to account for what he knows before anyone touches the rig again.

option_c.text: Take the fragment to Ilya’s side of the system and see what she is hiding.
option_c.next_session_opens: The next session opens with Nora carrying contraband proof into dangerous proximity with Amora, using Eli’s fragment as leverage.

session_end_choice.final_line: You have enough to choose. Not enough to wait.

q1_entry_point.detail: The session starts at the first live collision: Nora and Jace meeting under the beacon with active tether scramble and incoming footsteps. No passive setup precedes the first paragraph.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the first 300 words. Estimated count to S2_C1 setup/question: approximately 180-220 words from cold open through immediate threat framing.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices alter tracked state, NPC disposition, environmental flags, and cross-session payoff. Verified against Phase 6 consequence map and cross-session payoff plan: S2_C1 -> Sessions 3/5 bond and rescue texture; S2_C2 -> Sessions 3/5 lens symbolism and root-node use; S2_C3 -> Session 3 bodily-cost framing and later diver logic; S2_C4 -> Session 3 opening alliance posture.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes land before the first branch: Nora is hunted, bodily destabilized by an unauthorized tether, and forced to decide whether this stranger is threat or ally. The want is immediate survival plus control over what is happening to her body.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S2_C1 has named future payoff in Session 3 Events 51-53 and Session 5 Event 83; the design specifies visible later impact on Jace's intimacy, spacing, and rescue dynamics.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The designed Breath beat exists, but it is placed at 8:00-10:00 in the beat map, not between minutes 10-13 as required by this checklist. Phase 4/Session Architecture fails timing compliance. Revision: shift the analog-clutter/Jace dry-release/lens-wonder pocket later so the deliberate release lands inside 10:00-13:00 before the Twist compounds.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S2_C3 and presents no correct answer. Values in tension: bodily autonomy, truth under pressure, survival capacity.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The session architecture ends on S2_C4, but Session Close Design replaces that with a different end choice about Eli's fragment, Jace, and Ilya. This creates exit-state ambiguity between anticipation and structural confusion. Phase 7 fails end-hook consistency. Revision: unify the session-end choice so the player exits on one unresolved decision only, with the same hook in both Phase 5/6 choice design and Phase 7 close.

q10_talkability.detail: Multiple choices are immediately talkable, especially S2_C2 (what the lens becomes), S2_C3 (whether to submit to decode), and S2_C4 (stay by vow, utility, or commitment). A friend would ask what you chose.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned explanatory sci-fi dialogue appears in generated prose/design prose. Found: session_close_design.session_end_choice.option_c text and next_session_opens introduce 'Ilya's side of the system' and leverage framing in a more expository pitch register than the Voice Lock allows; multiple narrative setup/outcome lines also smooth into explanatory prose rather than clipped runtime voice. Phase 7 is the clearest failure point. Revision: rewrite the session-close choice text into compressed, embodied, conflict-bearing language that implies system stakes without explanatory phrasing.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: No disallowed action-line trailing simile constructions of the form 'like [metaphor]' were found in the generated prose.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: Forbidden mold appears repeatedly: 'Not X. Not Y. Just Z.' in S2_C1A ('Not trust. Not panic. Measurement.'), and balanced rule-of-three tricolon structures recur in multiple outcomes and setup lines. Phase 5/Choice Design fails mold protection. Revision: vary the corrective structures so they remain voice-authentic without repeating the exact banned scaffold.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open strongly matches author voice through at least two signature techniques: staccato fragment chaining ('Cold platform air. Wet concrete.') and perception-first reveal/body-tech coupling ('Same hand to the port... UNREGISTERED LINK / PROXIMITY SCRAMBLE'). Random outcomes also carry signature markers: S2_C2C uses object emotion tethering plus readout emotionalization; S2_C3C uses soft coercive procedural conflict and bodily-tech framing; S2_E4C uses exteriorized interiority through grip, sternum, cord, and restraint.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 voice-lock audit points pass. Flags: Point 6 Action Line Compression, Point 12 Emotional Restraint, and Point 14 Ending Decision Retention Hook due to overlong explanatory outcome prose and conflicting end-hook architecture between Phase 5/6 and Phase 7.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Core diction matches the profile: port, lens, chrome, glass, residue, integrity, reset, decode, warm, wrong, still. Nora stays clipped and boundary-driven; Jace remains dry and practical; Riven stays clinical and necessity-framed.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Phase 5 branching outcomes are frequently too long and syntactically smooth, especially S2_C1A, S2_C2A, S2_C3B, and S2_C4B.

Several explanatory summary lines name emotional interpretation too directly, especially in session architecture rationale and some outcome tails such as S2_C4C and session_close_design.resolution_prose.
Phase 7 session_close_design introduces a different final choice than S2_C4, splitting the retention hook.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Session Close Design introduces Ilya as an immediate next-step branch ('Take the fragment to Ilya's side of the system') that breaks the established Session 2->3 bridge, which should remain bunker/decode focused per session map. Phase 7 fails canon bridge integrity. Revision: replace the Ilya branch with a next-step that stays inside the confirmed Session 3 dramatic question about deeper decode cost, map completion, and alliance posture.

q18_character_truth.detail: Jace remains restrained, practical, and physically expressive rather than speechifying. Riven stays necessity-driven and accountable under pressure. No NPC behavior clearly violates StoryGuard character rules.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible: reflections intercept lawful routes, noisy breaches escalate Mirror deployment, readouts intensify bodily stakes, countdown pressure sharpens after safeguard delays, and NPC spacing/briefing posture changes based on player behavior.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State mismatch exists between Phase 5/6 and Phase 7 close. Phase 7 resolution prose states 'DECODE COMPLETE: 87%' and 'The cracked lens is still under your coat,' which conflicts with several Phase 5/6 paths where lens_decode_progress remains PARTIAL_THREAD and/or the lens is mounted in rig (S2_C2C). Revision: make Phase 7 close conditional by path, or normalize Phase 5/6 state so every route reaches the same lens location and decode-progress value before close.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant state changes are paired with in-world acknowledgments: spacing shifts, rig hum changes, kill switches exposed, countdown worsening, Jace's withdrawal or proximity, Riven's procedural recalibration. Signals are subtle, physical, and in voice.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices include three defining lines each. All are under 20 words, provocative, non-spoilery, and curiosity-generating.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Each branching choice spans lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no alignment is systematically advantaged in the design. Consequences differ in texture and cost rather than rewarding one register as the obvious best path.

revision_instructions.instruction: Move the Breath beat so the humor/wonder release lands between minutes 10-13 before the Twist.
Unify the ending so the session closes on one unresolved player-owned decision instead of conflicting end hooks.
Rewrite the session-close choice text into compressed, non-expository voice that implies system stakes without explanatory sci-fi phrasing.
Replace banned sentence molds, especially exact 'Not X. Not Y. Just Z.' scaffolds, with more varied voice-authentic constructions.

revision_instructions.phase: Phase 5/7 — Choice Design and Session Close
revision_instructions.instruction: Compress overlong outcome prose and reconcile the ending hook so all 14 voice-lock audit points pass.
Remove the Ilya-facing branch from the Session 2 close and restore a hook that bridges directly into Session 3's deeper decode/map arc.
Make close-state prose conditional on prior branch states, especially lens location and decode-progress value, so it matches Phase 5/6 persistence.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis:
The opening does not become retention-safe until the second decode begins to behave wrong. Everything before that is competent, atmospheric setup: bunker geography, resistance texture, Riven’s guilt disclosure, rig explanation, first decode aftermath, Ilya surveillance, recovery alcove quiet, and procedural prep for pass two. All of it is useful material. None of it gives a new user immediate playable tension inside the first minute on the page. The first paragraph cluster establishes mood and hiding place, not a present-tense dilemma. The first decode sequence carries bodily stakes, but in this session excerpt it arrives after substantial preamble and still plays more as technical process than immediate user-facing decision pressure. The Mirror visitation is emotionally strong, but it is followed by another recovery-and-setup stretch before the next active escalation. The first clear YES to the stakes test lands when Nora is already in the chair for the second pass and the decode comes through too clean. In that moment, a new user can instantly feel: your body is failing, the machine is behaving wrong, someone in the room knows it, and whatever is helping you may also be lying to you. That is dramatic tension without future knowledge.

So the cut should start at the second decode chamber beat, where the rig is live, the lens is on the plate, Nora is already committed, and the anomaly begins almost immediately. This cut removes passive onboarding and enters at the first moment where danger, mystery, and moral contamination are fused in action. The cut material is not discarded conceptually; it is redistributed. Nora’s damaged state, the cracked lens’s importance, Riven/Jace’s roles, and the fact that prior decode passes have already cost Nora physically must be reintroduced through tactile action, readouts, and clipped conflict lines in the cold open rather than through scene lead-in.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: INT. SITE THREE - DECODE CHAMBER - NIGHT - REALITY — second decode setup beginning with 'The rig. The chair. The cables.'
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 1,900 words before the cut across 5 scene blocks.
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Atmospheric bunker arrival, resistance hideout orientation, Riven confession/backstory, technical explanation of the rig, first decode procedure, Mirror visitation, Ilya monitoring beat, recovery alcove conversation, and second-pass procedural preamble.
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Nora is already physically damaged from earlier decode passes; the cracked lens is the key artifact and must stay intact; Riven is the operator with mission authority; Jace is present as watcher/protector; Nora is choosing to keep going despite port failure risk; the decode process is reading hidden data through analog damage.

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
Cold straps bite your wrists. Not restraints. Grounding points. The chair hums under your spine, metal and old current, while cracked glass throws a thin seam of light across the decode plate.

You are Nora. You do not wait for permission. You look at the lens. At the fracture. At the HUD ghosting at the edge of your vision: NEURAL PORT INTEGRITY: 4%. Then you say, "Go."

The rig answers low and rising. Cables pull taut. Light splits through the crack and sprays the chamber walls in broken geometry.

Too clean.

Not resistance. Not static. The data opens for you.

Riven goes still at the console. "Port degradation below threshold," she says. Wrong, in that flat voice that means worse than wrong.

Blood warms your upper lip. Your vision shears black for two seconds. Then back.

Inside the stream, something forms.

A lens. Perfect. Chrome-edged. Uncracked.

And through it, Eli says your name exactly the way the dead should not be able to.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: contamination. A user arrives feeling alarmed and wanting to decide what is real.

setup.source_moment: Cold-open cut at the second decode chamber: "The rig. The chair. The cables." Nora is already strapped into grounding points, the cracked lens is mounted, Riven says the second decode is on Nora's call, Nora answers "Go," and the data begins coming through too clean while Riven realizes the source has changed.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the first live dramatic tension in the excerpt after Phase 3 surgery. Location, situation, stakes, and agency are all legible immediately: Nora is in a dangerous decode, her port is failing, the lens matters, and something in the stream is helping in a way it should not. It satisfies the opening protocol because the player understands where they are, what is happening, and why their input matters before the 3-minute mark. Branching choice #1 can land on how Nora approaches the wrong-clean decode inside the first 300 words.

escalation.source_moment: The second decode continues: Riven notes degradation is slower than the first pass and therefore wrong; Nora suffers blackout and vision-cortex spike; then the stream manifests a perfect uncracked chrome lens and Eli's exact voice. In parallel, the earlier first-pass aftermath, recovery alcove, and Mirror trace are redistributed as compressed context and consequence.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is to complete the map cleanly enough to get the remaining decode truth before Nora's port fails. The clear obstacle is that the decode is now contaminated by the Mirror using Eli's residue, which makes the data easier to access but morally and operationally suspect. Stakes compound on three axes at once: bodily cost, truth reliability, and grief vulnerability.

breath.source_moment: Recovery alcove material centered on Jace and the recorder: his dry observational 'field report' about blankets, pipe condensation, and 'The diver sleeps with one eye open,' followed by Nora's flat correction: 'That's not a field report.' 'Force of habit.'
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's pressure-release pocket. It offers bleak irony, human texture, and fragile intimacy without dissolving the danger. It also rewards the player with relational warmth and observational detail between minutes 8 and 10, satisfying the Schell Rule. The humor is exactly this author's kind: dry, brief, and still under pressure.

twist.source_moment: Mirror contact reframed inside and around the second pass: the system can complete the thread through Eli's residue with less bodily cost, and the apparition is not a crude fake but a truth-bearing contamination. Nora must decide whether to use the wrong-clean channel, slow or interrupt it, or refuse the assistance even if that threatens the mission.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is moral-weight because every option costs something real. Accepting help may finish the map while surrendering autonomy and opening the channel wider. Refusing preserves the real and resists coercion but may destroy Nora's body or lose mission-critical data. Interrogating the stream protects truth standards but risks timing under the Reset. There is no correct answer because body, mission, and grief are all legitimate claims.

resolution.source_moment: The decode completes 'clean. Too clean.' The hidden Mirror trace pulses, and the session ends not on aftermath but on the unresolved next-step decision: whether Nora reports the contamination and commits to repair/containment, conceals it and pushes toward the strike, or uses the trace as leverage against the Core route.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The apparent goal resolved is immediate map progress: the second pass yields major decode advancement and confirms the full-map path is now reachable. The seed for next session is planted in the unresolved consequence of contamination plus bodily cost: Nora now has actionable truth and a compromised port, which naturally leads into Session 4's question of who she believes about the system and what destroying it will cost.

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Identity - self_preservation_vs_sacrifice. Does Nora treat the wrong-clean second pass as a chance to push harder, or as a danger that must be constrained?
branching_choices.dramatic_question: The data opens too easily. Do you drive the decode forward, demand safeguards before continuing, or interrogate the anomaly before giving it more of your port?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Methodology - preserve_evidence_vs_self_protection. What is the cracked lens to Nora right now: relic, evidence, or tool?
branching_choices.dramatic_question: When the stream starts behaving like Eli, do you protect the cracked lens as his last real trace, press Riven to prove what the data contains, or weaponize the lens as the only way through?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Moral Weight - autonomy_vs_compliance. The Mirror offers completion without added pain, but only if Nora opens the channel it is already exploiting.
branching_choices.dramatic_question: Do you let the contaminated channel carry you to the full map, refuse it outright and accept more bodily cost, or use the contact while denying it any promise of consent?

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Future Commitment - memory_continuity_vs_functional_survival. This is the session-end hook that sets up repair, clean-port vulnerability, and Session 4's ideological pressure.
branching_choices.dramatic_question: Now that the second pass is complete and the trace is inside you, what do you commit to next: report the contamination and preserve function, hide it and keep the residue advantage, or force one more push before anyone can clean the port?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Nora asking Jace to stay in the room because she needs someone who is not running the machine.
Nora's handling of the integrity readout and her refusal to be pulled early if data is coming through.
Jace's recorder 'field report' about condensation, blankets, and the diver sleeping with one eye open.
The silence after 'Recording things I need to remember is,' where Nora can either let the intimacy stand or shut it down.
The blackout and vision loss during the second pass when Nora calls for Riven and the room resolves again.

Manifestation of the perfect chrome lens and Eli-voice inside the stream.

can_be_reshaped: Riven's technical explanation of port degradation thresholds and residue pattern logic.
Jace's practical concern about Nora's hand, hearing, and visible symptoms.

pressure_points.source_moment: Opening anomaly in the second decode chamber when the data comes through too clean and Riven says, 'Slower is wrong.'
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Clinical dread and immediate suspicion.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can observe, brace, or intensify, which colors Nora's stance toward danger without changing the decode's necessity.

pressure_points.source_moment: Blackout, blood, and the first appearance of the perfect chrome lens carrying Eli's voice.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Fear fused with grief-recognition; wrong-familiar seduction.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can recoil or lean in, revealing whether Nora treats the apparition as threat, evidence, or temptation while the stream continues.

pressure_points.source_moment: Recovery alcove exchange with Jace and the recorder.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Tenderness, embarrassment, and dry humor under exhaustion.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can deflect or soften, shaping relational tone while preserving the alcove's function as a breath beat and reset window.

pressure_points.source_moment: Late twist and aftermath when the contaminated decode completes and Nora decides whether to report or conceal the trace.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Secrecy, shame, tactical calculation, and mission urgency.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can withdraw, harden, or stay clinical, which affects trust texture for Session 4 without interrupting the forward drive toward the Core.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: This session must plant that Nora now has actionable map progress, a compromised but still useful port, and an unresolved contamination secret that makes Ilya's later clean-port contact and the city's destabilization feel inevitable rather than abrupt.

branching_choices.source_moment: Second decode activation. The data comes through clean when it should grind. Riven sees the degradation rate and knows the source has changed.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The rig hums under you. Old needles. Green pulse. The cracked lens throws fractured light across the chamber wall. Then the stream opens too easily, and Riven goes still at the console.
branching_choices.choice_question: The data opens wrong-clean. How do you meet it?

options.label: A
options.text: You make Riven read the anomaly aloud before you give the stream one inch more of your port.
options.outcome: Your voice cuts through the hum before the room can pretend this is ordinary. Riven does not argue. Does not soothe. She reads the numbers flat. Degradation below expected rate. Channel separation too stable. Source variance unconfirmed. Jace shifts off the wall at that. Closer. Not close. The rig keeps running while the truth enters the room in pieces. Not safer. Just named. Your port stays warm under the collarbone. Wrong-familiar. The cracked lens trembles in its cradle as if the fracture itself objects. Riven recalibrates with tighter filters and a narrower translation gate. You buy seconds. Not mercy. When the stream hits again, everyone is watching it as contamination, not miracle. That matters.
options.downstream_effect: Riven narrows the decode gate and the chamber enters a more clinical, evidence-first posture.

npc_dispositions.shift: respect for operational scrutiny +1
trust in Nora's caution +1
access slowed by resistance framing -1

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Tested the wrong-clean stream before yielding.
key_successes_failures: Named contamination early.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven's fingers change rhythm on the calibration board. Jace stops looking at you and starts looking at the machine.
options.defining_line: You do not trust relief that arrives too fast.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell Riven to push the decode now and take whatever the stream is willing to give.
options.outcome: You do not wait for permission or proof. Just the word. Push. It lands hard in the chamber. Riven's jaw locks once and she obeys the instruction she would never give herself. Gain rises. Filters loosen. The fractured light off the lens sharpens into cleaner bands across the concrete. Too clean. Your port answers like flesh recognizing a blade it has already met. Heat under the scar. Vision thinning at the edges. But the data comes. Fast. Ordered. Greedy. Jace comes off the wall with a curse he does not finish. Riven keeps the channel open one beat longer than protocol. One beat because you asked. One beat that gets you deeper and lets something else get deeper too. The room smells faintly metallic. Blood and hot dust.
options.downstream_effect: The decode accelerates immediately, yielding more data but widening the contaminated channel.

npc_dispositions.shift: regard for Nora's burn-rate resolve +1
alarm at Nora's self-endangerment +1
access increased through opened channel +1

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: Spent more port integrity to force progress.
defining_moral_choices: Chose truth speed over bodily caution.

options.world_noticed_signal: The oscilloscope pulse climbs and holds where it should shudder. Jace says nothing after the first step forward.
options.defining_line: You spend body first and ask what it cost after.

options.label: C
options.text: You order Riven to lock safeguards before the second pass goes any deeper.
options.outcome: You do not raise your voice. You do not need to. Lock it down. Riven hears the order in the same register she uses herself. Gain drops. Translation narrows. A hard limiter slides across the readout and the room changes with it. Less velocity. More drag. More honest resistance. The data no longer pours. It grinds. Your port cools by degrees, then flares again when the stream pushes back against the constraint. Not gone. Never gone. Just contained badly. Jace exhales through his nose. Not relief. Recognition. Riven watches the integrity line like she is waiting for it to lie. The cracked lens throws a smaller light now, tighter and meaner. You may lose fragments this way. You may keep more of yourself. For the moment, those are not the same thing.
options.downstream_effect: Safety protocols engage, reducing immediate speed and forcing the anomaly to work against visible constraints.

npc_dispositions.shift: friction at being constrained, respect for discipline +1
confidence in Nora's self-protection +1
frustrated by narrowed access -1

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Set conditions on further bodily risk.
defining_moral_choices: Chose containment over speed.

options.world_noticed_signal: A new limiter tone joins the chamber hum. Even the light through the lens looks tighter, less willing.
options.defining_line: You make danger come through a door, not a flood.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The blackout and vision-cortex spike as the stream reveals the source is helping.

canon_boundaries: Each decode pass degrades port integrity and carries real sensory cost.
The cracked analog lens is required to decode the hidden thread.
The Mirror can exploit residue already present in Nora's port but cannot be treated as harmless help.
No choice cancels the necessity of the decode or removes contamination risk.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: She answers pressure with procedure, not comfort; if challenged, she tightens the rig or pushes it harder, but never gets sentimental.
He tracks bodily risk in concrete terms and steps closer when Nora endangers herself, but keeps language spare.
It uses any opening as invitation and responds to suspicion by becoming more exact, not louder.

available_objects: cracked camera lens in dock mount
scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Riven knows the readouts are wrong but not the full extent of the hidden trace; Jace can read danger, not source architecture; Nora feels contamination before she can prove it.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Clinical dread. Wrong-clean relief. Everyone understands the machine is behaving too well.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: The decode continues with altered framing and lands in the same blackout/anomaly reveal.
fold_back_path.why: The session spine requires the second pass to proceed and expose the contaminated assistance.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Player pushes the machine, overrides caution, or dares the anomaly to show itself faster.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Player imposes safeguards, asks for controlled procedure, or slows the pass to preserve function.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Player interrogates the readout, demands evidence, or withholds consent until the anomaly is named.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: Act vs test vs contain under immediate technical wrongness.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: S3:E45 - Nora's willingness to spend her own body to finish what she started.

branching_choices.source_moment: The stream begins carrying Eli-signature warmth. The perfect chrome lens appears inside the decode while the cracked lens remains on the plate.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Inside the stream, a perfect lens hangs where no real lens should be. Warm light. Even edges. Eli's exact register through something that has never been touched by a bathroom tile or your hands. On the plate below, the cracked lens keeps catching sodium light like a wound that learned to hold data.
branching_choices.choice_question: When the stream starts behaving like Eli, what do you make the lens become?

options.label: A
options.text: You protect the cracked lens as evidence and force Riven to work around what must stay real.
options.outcome: You do not reach for the perfect thing. You anchor on the damaged one. Real glass. Real fracture. The cord rough against your fingers when Riven lifts it free between passes. You make the rule plain: no aggressive remount, no forced gain, no using the lens like disposable hardware. Evidence first. Riven hates the delay because delay has a body count attached to it. She obeys anyway. Jace watches your hand close over the lens and understands exactly what line you are drawing. Not sentiment. Chain of custody. The crack is not weakness here. It is proof. The next calibration is slower and uglier. Less elegant. More honest. The Mirror's imitation loses some of its leverage when the room treats the real object as testimony instead of bait.
options.downstream_effect: The lens is handled under Nora's terms, slowing procedure and strengthening its status as protected evidence.

npc_dispositions.shift: frustrated by delay but grants more operational respect +1
sees Nora preserving the real rather than chasing comfort +1
reduced emotional leverage through object control -1

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Preserved the cracked lens as proof.
secrets_discovered: Treated the anomaly as contamination against material evidence.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven starts reaching for the lens only after looking at you first. The room begins treating the cradle like an evidence tray, not a weapon rack.
options.defining_line: You keep the proof scarred enough to stay true.

options.label: B
options.text: You press Riven to prove what in the stream is data and what is grief trying on your dead.
options.outcome: You refuse the false choice between reverence and surrender. Prove it. Riven splits the feed. Labels channels. One carrying map structure. One carrying residue pattern. She does it fast, voice clipped, hands cleaner now that she has a problem she can name. Jace stays near your left side where the hearing still ghosts in and out. The perfect chrome lens remains in the stream, but thinner. Less offer. More specimen. Eli's voice still lands where it hurts. That does not make it authority. You make the room study the contamination instead of kneeling to it or throwing it away. The cost is time. The gain is distinction. Not certainty. Never that. But enough to keep moving without pretending the stream and your grief are the same thing.

One more bad pass and it might not stay intact. Jace says your name once. Not loud. Enough. You do not look at him. The stream answers violence with clarity. More map. More structure. More of Eli's warmth riding inside the channel like a hand in a glove. That is the bargain. Not spoken. Felt. The lens gives more when treated like a blade, and something in the system learns that you will spend what remains of him if the route opens.

branching_choices.source_moment: The Mirror offers to complete the thread through Eli's residue with less bodily cost if Nora opens the channel it is already exploiting.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The warmth in your port deepens. Not memory. Not hallucination. A live channel wearing the shape of what you miss most. Riven can see the numbers. Only you can feel how little pain the offer promises.
branching_choices.choice_question: The contaminated channel can finish the map with less damage. What do you do with the offer?

options.label: A
options.text: You open the channel and take the full map now before your body can fail first.
options.outcome: You stop fighting the contact and let it in. Not surrender. Not that word. Access. The shift is immediate. The port heat settles instead of spiking. Signal settles. The cleanest channel you have felt since before Eli died. Coordinates flood in whole. Hub designations. Route layers. A kill map unfolding without the tearing resistance of the first pass. Riven sees the integrity line stabilize and goes pale in a way she will deny later. Jace pushes off the wall, already knowing ease is the wrong symptom. Eli's voice does not speak again. It does not need to. The help is the voice now. You get what you wanted. Fast. Almost gentle. And when the pass ends, something of the channel does not withdraw with it. The room is still the room. Your port is not.
options.downstream_effect: The map completes with reduced immediate bodily damage, but the Mirror secures a deeper live foothold in Nora's port.

fear of Nora drifting into the system +1

sacrifices_made: Traded autonomy risk for reduced bodily damage.
key_successes_failures: Secured full map through compromised channel.

options.world_noticed_signal: The oscilloscope stops fighting and begins to sing in one clean line. Riven says your name too late to matter.
options.defining_line: You let the wrong thing carry you because the truth still matters.

options.label: B
options.text: You refuse the contaminated help and make the decode finish the hard way.
options.outcome: No. The word lands like a seal. You shut the offer out and the stream turns ugly at once. Static shear. Heat spike. Your port bucks under the scar as if the machine resents being denied the easier path. Riven has to widen manual control and ride the degradation line by hand. Jace is beside the chair now, not touching, close enough to catch you if your body decides before you do. The map still comes, but in torn pieces that have to be held by force. Real cost. Real pain. Blood at the nose again. Left hand gone numb to the wrist for three long beats. When the decode completes, the data is yours without the Mirror's clean consent-shaped mercy. The channel cools. Mostly. Not fully. Refusal protected the line. It did not erase the trace.
options.downstream_effect: The decode completes through raw strain, preserving Nora's refusal while inflicting sharper bodily cost and leaving only a residual trace.

protective loyalty intensified +1

options.world_noticed_signal: The room fills with the sound of the rig working too hard. Jace does not speak again until you are breathing on your own rhythm.
options.defining_line: You would rather break honestly than be carried clean.

options.label: C
options.text: You use the contaminated channel for the map while denying it any promise beyond the data.
options.outcome: You split the difference and refuse to call it surrender. Fine. Data only. The words are for you as much as for it. Riven hears enough to adjust the rig around a partial opening. Jace hears enough to hate the shape of the compromise without interrupting it. The channel enters in pulses instead of flood. Clean bursts. Then drag. Then warmth again. You take what it gives and cut it off each time before it settles into comfort. The map assembles in staggered clarity. Slower than full acceptance. Safer than full refusal. Not safe. Never that. By the end, Riven has the route and you still have your line, if a line can survive being crossed in measured increments. The trace remains active. Controlled, for now. Like a wound packed tight around shrapnel.
options.downstream_effect: The map completes through a controlled partial opening, leaving Nora with usable data and an active but managed contamination channel.

branching_choices.narrative_setup: The chamber is concrete again. Flat green hum. Blood drying under your nose. Riven has the route she needed, and your port stays warm where it should be cold.
branching_choices.choice_question: Now that the map is real and the trace is inside you, what do you commit to next?

options.label: A
options.text: You report the contamination and commit to repair before the next move toward the Core.
options.outcome: You say it before the silence can harden into concealment. The trace is in your port. Riven's face shuts down into math. Jace's does not. He had already guessed enough to fear the answer. Reporting it changes the room at once. Not softer. Cleaner. Riven starts talking in thresholds, surgical windows, what can be cut, what will be lost. You know the cost before she names it. Clean port. Dead channels. Whatever residue of Eli still lives in the hardware gone with the corrupted substrate. Functional survival bought by subtraction. The map stays. The warmth does not. Jace does not reach for you. He stays. Which is worse. The next decision is no longer whether to tell them. It is whether you can survive being repaired into someone emptier and still keep moving.
options.downstream_effect: Riven pivots the team toward repair planning, making residue loss and functional survival the immediate next-session problem.

options.label: B
options.text: You hide the contamination and keep the residue advantage for the strike.
options.outcome: You keep your mouth shut and let the room believe the warmth is only aftermath. Riven has the map. Jace has your face. Neither has the whole truth. Concealment buys you one thing: time with the residue still alive in your hardware. Time the Mirror can use. Time you can use first, if you are faster. The lie is not dramatic. That would be easier. It is procedural. A missing sentence. A swallowed warning. Riven starts planning the next move toward the Core with your compromised port still inside the equation and not fully named. Jace watches you too long before looking away. He knows something is off. Not what. Not yet. The trace pulses once under the scar. Answering the silence. The next session opens with a secret in your body and a mission built on it.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace's eyes stay on you a second too long. The port answers your silence with one low pulse you feel and no one else hears.
options.defining_line: You keep the ghost because it is still useful.
options.next_session_opens: Low corridor light. The team moving on your map while the hidden trace warms under your skin like a second route.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep the contamination quiet for now and force one more controlled use of the trace before anyone can clean it.
options.outcome: You do not confess. You do not simply hide. You convert the problem into a plan. One more pass. Controlled. Limited. Before repair, before panic, before anyone decides for you what must be cut away. Riven hears only the part about needing time to stabilize and accepts it because the map has changed the clock. Jace does not accept it. He files it. The trace in your port feels less like warmth now and more like pressure behind glass. Usable. Dangerous. Finite. You are not preserving Eli. You are not surrendering to the Mirror. You are treating contamination as leverage while you still can. That distinction may be real. It may be one more lie with better posture. Next session begins with preparation, secrecy, and the question of whether you are managing the channel or being taught by it.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Nora tells Jace to stay in the decode chamber because she needs someone who is not running the machine.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Jace stops in the doorway. The recorder presses against his ribs under the jacket. You hear your own need after it is already in the room.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you let him stay?

options.label: A
options.text: You tell him to watch the machine, not you.
options.outcome: He nods once like you handed him a task instead of trust. Good. Cleaner that way. He shifts to the wall with his eyes on the console, on the cables, on the places a body can fail before it says so. Riven notices the redistribution and says nothing. The room tightens into procedure again, but not all the way. Jace is still here. Still the one person in the chamber not speaking in thresholds and percentages. When your hand starts to shake against the armrest, he sees it because he was told to see everything except the part that matters most.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays clipped and procedural, with intimacy routed through task assignment.

npc_dispositions.shift: accepts distance but hears trust underneath +1

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Asked Jace to stay as witness and safeguard.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace plants himself where he can see both your face and Riven's hands.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell him you need a second set of eyes if the room goes wrong.
options.outcome: That lands closer to the truth and farther from confession. Jace takes it that way. Practical. Shared perimeter. He moves in without making the moment larger than it is. Riven glances between you once, files it, returns to the board. The chamber keeps its old-metal hum. Your pulse does not. Jace stays angled toward the rig, but every few seconds his attention checks back to you like a field instrument verifying a signal. Not tenderness. Not only that. A second witness. A second measure. Enough human presence to keep the machine from owning the whole room.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans toward shared vigilance and measured interdependence.

npc_dispositions.shift: partnership tone strengthened +1

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Let Jace function as partner under pressure.

options.world_noticed_signal: He stops leaning and starts tracking the room like it might turn on both of you.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell him not to let them pull you unless you say the word.
options.outcome: The line comes out hard enough to sound like an order and private enough to sound like a plea. Jace hears both. His mouth tightens once. Riven definitely hears the instruction and definitely does not like it. Good. The chamber shifts around the new fault line. Your body versus their thresholds. Your word versus protocol. Jace stays because leaving would be agreement with the machine. He stays and takes the burden of being the person you asked to choose wrong with you if it comes to that. The hum of the rig does not change. The room does.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens into defiance and contested bodily authority.

npc_dispositions.shift: protective burden increased +1
irritation at Nora's anti-protocol demand +1

emotional_choices.source_moment: After the first pass, Nora swipes away the port integrity warning and refuses to explain visible symptoms.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The HUD says 4%. Your left ear is a high thin wire. Jace says your hand like the word might still reach it.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you answer the readout and the concern under it?

options.label: A
options.text: You tell them the number matters less than what it bought.
options.outcome: You make the trade sound simple on purpose. Number for data. Pain for route. Riven understands that language too well to challenge it honestly. Jace does not. His face closes down into something flatter than anger. The HUD vanishes under your hand and the gesture says more than the sentence. You are not interested in being managed by percentages now that the machine has proven it can still yield. The room lets you keep that posture because no one has a better answer ready. The cost remains visible anyway. Tremor. Ringing. The small lag before your fingers obey. Bodies keep records even when you refuse the paperwork.
options.tonal_effect: Narration hardens around cost accounting and refusal of pity.

npc_dispositions.shift: reads Nora as mission-burn operator +1
worry sharpened by Nora's dismissal +1

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: Minimized bodily damage in favor of data gain.

options.world_noticed_signal: The warning disappears. The tremor does not.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell Jace exactly what you can no longer hear and move on.
options.outcome: You give him the report because it is useful, not because it is intimate. Left side. Ringing. Intermittent drop. Hand lag. Jace takes the information cleanly and stores it where he stores routes, exits, and other things that keep people alive. Riven adjusts nothing in her face, but her next glance at the readout lasts longer. Naming the damage does not make the room gentle. It makes it accurate. That is closer to mercy here. Your symptoms stop being deniable and start being shared fact. The rig, the lens, the next pass. All of it now has a body attached in language, not only in consequence.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes more clinical and exact, with vulnerability translated into usable facts.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust raised by plain reporting +1
risk assessment sharpened by Nora's candor +1

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Shared actionable damage report with allies.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace stops asking if you are fine. He starts tracking the left side of you as a changing condition.

options.label: B
options.text: You give them nothing except that you are still here.
options.outcome: Still here is not reassurance. It is inventory. Jace hears the difference. So does Riven. Neither presses because both of them know pressure can waste what little steadiness you have left before the next pass. The silence that follows is not empty. It fills with the hum of the powered-down rig, the faint needle tick, the sound of people choosing not to force a door. You keep the details in your own body for now. Private data. Private damage. The room adjusts around that choice by watching harder instead of asking louder.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays withheld, with attention moving from speech to observation.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Jace records the alcove like a field report: condensation, blankets, and the diver sleeping with one eye open.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Pipe condensation. Three-second intervals. Emergency blanket smell. Jace gets caught pretending he is documenting infrastructure instead of you.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you answer the fake field report?

options.label: A
options.text: You ask him if all his reports are this bad.
options.outcome: The line lands dry enough to survive the room. Jace almost smiles. Almost is enough. He turns the recorder in his hand like it might defend him better than words do. Bad habit, he says, and the answer is half joke, half confession, which is about as open as he gets without bleeding. The alcove loosens by one degree. Not warmth exactly. More like pressure redistributing. The pipes keep their rhythm overhead. The HUD keeps counting down. But for a moment the place is not only damage control. It is two exhausted people noticing each other without asking the scene to become kinder than it is.
options.tonal_effect: Narration allows a brief dry lift without breaking severity.

npc_dispositions.shift: ease and rapport +1

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Shared pressure-release humor with Jace.

options.world_noticed_signal: The corner of Jace's mouth moves and then thinks better of it.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell him to stop recording if he wants the subject to live.
options.outcome: It comes out flatter than a joke and meaner than a threat. Which is why it works. Jace blinks once, then lets out a breath that could almost count as a laugh if either of you were in a different life. He palms the recorder and says nothing for a beat. The silence that follows is lighter for having survived the hit. Not safe. Not soft. Just briefly human in a room built for attrition. Even the blanket scratch against your wrist seems less hostile for a second. Then the port warms again under your skin and the moment closes before it can turn into comfort.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens with gallows humor and immediate recoil back into unease.

npc_dispositions.shift: drawn closer by Nora's dry aggression +1

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Used humor to hold off fear.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace lowers the recorder but does not put it away. Not yet.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell him that is not a field report and leave it there.
options.outcome: You keep the correction plain. Enough to name what he is doing. Not enough to punish it. Jace accepts the hit with a small nod and the practiced answer. Force of habit. The phrase lands between you like a tool set down carefully. Neither of you reaches for more. The alcove stays quiet after that. Good quiet. Not empty. The kind that lets two people remain in the same hard place without pretending it is easier than it is. The recorder goes still in his hand. The pipes keep breathing overhead. The reward here is not laughter. It is the fact that the room holds.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays restrained and intimate through correction rather than banter.

npc_dispositions.shift: respects Nora's restraint +1

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Allowed closeness without inviting explanation.

options.world_noticed_signal: The recorder goes quiet. The silence after it does not feel hostile.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The silence after Jace explains recording things he needs to remember.

emotional_choices.source_moment: After Jace says recording things he needs to remember is a habit, the sentence sits between them.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: He does not pick the sentence back up. Neither do you. The blanket scratches at your wrist. The port stays warm under the skin.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What do you do with the silence?

options.label: A
options.text: You let the silence stand and do not make him defend it.
options.outcome: You leave the line where he set it. No demand. No rescue. Jace looks at the recorder once, then away, and the mercy of not being pressed registers in his shoulders before it ever reaches his face. The alcove quiet stays intact. Thin, but intact. You can hear the pipe drip. You can hear the HUD numbers falling. You can hear your own left ear trying and failing to become normal again. None of it breaks the moment. The tenderness here is not spoken. It is the absence of intrusion. The choice not to make a person explain why remembering has become a survival skill.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows gentler through omission and preserved space.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust deepened by unforced quiet +1

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy +1

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace stops turning the recorder in his hand. He lets it rest.

options.label: B
options.text: You ask him what he is afraid of forgetting.
options.outcome: The question is quiet enough not to count as pressure and sharp enough to count as real. Jace does not answer fast. That tells you more than the first word would have. When it comes, it is small. Details, maybe. Voices. The sort of things systems flatten first. He does not elaborate and you do not force him to. Still, the alcove changes. Not open. Not healed. Just more inhabited. The recorder in his hand stops being a habit and becomes evidence of a private war against disappearance. Yours is not the only one in this room.
options.tonal_effect: Narration shifts toward mutual recognition through precise questioning.

npc_dispositions.shift: emotional access opened slightly +1

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: Saw Jace's archive habit as fear of loss.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace answers without looking at you. Which is answer enough.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell him that sounds like a bad way to stay alive.
options.outcome: It is harsher than comfort and closer than dismissal. Jace takes it without flinching. Bad habit, he says again, but this time the dryness has weight under it. The exchange does not open into confession. It opens into recognition of damage. Yours. His. Different hardware. Same problem. The alcove keeps its shape, but something in it becomes less solitary. Not because either of you said the right thing. Because neither of you bothered pretending remembering is noble. It is just one more way to keep the dead from being processed into something easier.
options.tonal_effect: Narration keeps tenderness under abrasion, refusing softness while allowing closeness.

npc_dispositions.shift: bond through shared damage language +1

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Rejected sentimental framing of memory.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace's answer comes back smaller the second time. More true.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The breath beat curdles back into unease as Nora feels the trace still present.

emotional_choices.source_moment: After the moral choice, the decode completes and the room has to absorb what Nora accepted, refused, or managed.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The pass ends. The map is there. So is the aftertaste of how you got it. Blood. Flat green hum. Jace and Riven both waiting to see which part of you comes back first.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What do you let them see in the first second after the decode?

options.label: A
options.text: You ask for the readout before you say anything else.
options.outcome: Riven gives it to you immediately because numbers are easier than faces. Integrity. Drop rate. Residue activity. Channel behavior. You take the report like a railing. Jace watches that choice and understands it for what it is: not calm, exactly. Structure. Something to hold before feeling catches up. The chamber follows your lead and becomes technical for one more minute. Useful. Temporary. Fear does not leave. It just gets translated into labels and thresholds long enough for you to stand inside it without naming it outright.
options.tonal_effect: Narration routes fear through procedure and measurement.

npc_dispositions.shift: meets Nora on technical ground +1

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: Reached for structure before emotion.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven starts reading numbers before anyone asks if you are all right.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell them not to touch you yet.
options.outcome: The words come fast enough to prove you were already braced for hands. Jace stops where he is. Riven never started moving. Good. The boundary changes the room more than a shout would have. It says whatever just happened inside your port is still happening, and your body is yours until it is not. Fear sharpens into distance. Useful distance. Jace takes the hit and stays anyway. Not close. Still here. Riven returns to the console because machines are easier than people who have just come back wrong from a channel. The moment buys you space. It also announces how little of it you feel you have.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns fear into hard perimeter and bodily control.

npc_dispositions.shift: respects boundary, worry intensified +1

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Asserted bodily boundary immediately after contamination.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace freezes on command. The space around the chair feels suddenly measured in inches.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell Jace you can stand if he gives you one second.
options.outcome: You do not deny the need. You regulate it. One second. Jace nods and waits exactly that long, which is maybe the most intimate thing either of you has done all night. Riven looks away on purpose and gives the second back to you untouched. Fear stays in the room, but it is no longer ownerless. It has shape now. Time. Count. Contact delayed but not refused. When you rise, whether steady or not, the chamber absorbs the choice as discipline rather than collapse. That distinction matters to everyone in it, including you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration frames fear as managed vulnerability and controlled reliance.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust through precise dependence +1

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Allowed help under self-set terms.

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace counts with you without saying the numbers out loud.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The chamber settles into resolution and the unresolved contamination decision.

posture_shifts.placement: Opening anomaly in the decode chamber when the data comes through too clean.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The stream opens like a door that was supposed to stick.

options.label: A
options.text: You watch the readout, not the light.
options.narration_adjustment: Numbers first. Rate drop. Channel separation. The wrongness arrives as pattern before it becomes fear.

options.label: B
options.text: You brace against the chair and wait.
options.narration_adjustment: Your shoulders lock before your thoughts do. Metal under skin. Breath measured small. You meet danger by holding still enough to hear it coming.

options.label: C
options.text: You lean toward the cleaner signal.
options.narration_adjustment: The ease of it catches you. Not trust. Not yet. But the part of you that needs the map moves first.

posture_shifts.placement: Immediate aftermath of Riven saying slower is wrong.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Riven's voice changes by half a degree, and the room follows.

options.label: A
options.text: You push your chin up and stay with it.
options.narration_adjustment: No retreat in the posture. The machine can be wrong. You can still be willing. Jace notices that before Riven does.

options.label: B
options.text: You go very still and listen harder.
options.narration_adjustment: Stillness as instrument. The hum separates into parts. One of them does not belong.

posture_shifts.placement: Blackout and vision-cortex spike during the second pass.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The light goes out inside your head before the room has time to react.

options.label: A
options.text: You reach for the armrest and hold.
options.narration_adjustment: Grip first. Thought later. The strap bites your palm and gives the body one true thing to answer.

options.label: B
options.text: You wait for the room to come back.
options.narration_adjustment: No thrash. No blind grab. Just the count between dark and return. Riven sees the control and hates what it costs.

options.label: C
options.text: You force yourself to track the channel anyway.
options.narration_adjustment: Even blind, you keep hunting the signal. Coordinates over panic. Route over body for one more beat.

posture_shifts.placement: First appearance of the perfect chrome lens and Eli-voice inside the stream.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The voice arrives wearing warmth your body remembers before your mind permits it.

options.label: A
options.text: You pull back from the warmth.
options.narration_adjustment: The recognition hits and you answer it with distance. Not him. The correction is physical before it is verbal.

options.label: B
options.text: You study what makes it almost right.
options.narration_adjustment: Cadence. Pause. Register. You catalog the lie by the truth inside it. That is its own kind of closeness.

posture_shifts.placement: Jace's recorder field report in the recovery alcove.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: His voice stays low enough to pretend the recorder is the one being addressed.

options.label: A
options.text: You let the dryness cover the room.
options.narration_adjustment: The joke stays thin and useful. Enough to cut pressure. Not enough to invite softness you cannot carry.

options.label: B
options.text: You hear the care inside it.
options.narration_adjustment: The report is not about pipes. You know it. He knows you know it. Neither of you makes the truth louder than it has to be.

posture_shifts.placement: The silence after Jace says he records things he needs to remember.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The sentence stays between you like something fragile and badly stored.

options.label: A
options.text: You leave it untouched.
options.narration_adjustment: No pressure added. No explanation demanded. The silence does the work and survives it.

options.label: B
options.text: You turn toward him a fraction.
options.narration_adjustment: Barely movement. Enough. The alcove notices. So does he.

options.label: C
options.text: You look back at the HUD instead.
options.narration_adjustment: Numbers are easier than archive habits. The countdown accepts your attention without asking what it costs.

posture_shifts.placement: The Mirror's low-cost offer inside the second pass.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: No pain. No cost. The words arrive in a voice built to bypass argument.

options.label: A
options.text: You harden against the tenderness.
options.narration_adjustment: The softness makes you angrier than threat would. Care used as leverage is still leverage.

options.label: B
options.text: You listen for the hidden price.
options.narration_adjustment: Every easy offer has a receipt. You start looking for where it will be collected.

posture_shifts.placement: After the decode completes too cleanly and Nora chooses whether to report or conceal the trace.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The warmth under the scar does not leave with the machine noise.

options.label: A
options.text: You keep your face clinically blank.
options.narration_adjustment: No flinch. No touch to the neck. If there is a secret, it will have to live without help from your body.

options.label: B
options.text: You touch the port before you stop yourself.
options.narration_adjustment: Too late to make it nothing. Jace sees. Riven may have. The body has already filed its own report.

options.label: C
options.text: You look at the lens, not at them.
options.narration_adjustment: Cracked glass. Real fracture. Easier to hold that than the room. Easier to ask the object what comes next.

available_objects: cracked camera lens mounted on decode plate

canon_boundaries_active: Each decode pass degrades port integrity.
The cracked analog lens is required for correct decode.
Reflections and channels remain potential Mirror access vectors while system is live.

Player demanding lore exposition about the Mirror or SoulSync mechanics

present_npcs.current_disposition: closer, protective, skeptical of machine ease

scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Fear fused with recognition. Grief object and contaminated data become the same immediate problem.
canon_boundaries_active: The Mirror can generate convincing simulations but not flawless real replacements.
Technical language must stay tied to bodily cost.
No option permits consequence-free contamination.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempting to speak to Eli as if fully real
Player trying to cure the contamination on the spot
Player asking Riven for extended exposition mid-pass
