pressure_points.source_moment: Ilya’s clean-port contact under the overpass, especially when she reframes Eli’s lingering trace as love.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Violation, grief, ideological seduction, and the need to control visible reaction in front of Jace and Riven.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Short posture options let the player harden, conceal hurt, or answer with sharpened contempt while the scene still drives toward the same core exchange and signal cutoff.

pressure_points.source_moment: The transit corridor and public grief failures: frozen billboard, dark kiosks, crying woman, stopped pedestrian.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Curiosity and dread. The player wants to register what the city is becoming without stopping the mission.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Observation-based posture shifts let the player decide whether Nora reads the collapse clinically, morally, or personally while preserving forward motion toward the plant.

pressure_points.source_moment: Jace revealing the chrome spread and admitting his echo log now has static.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Tenderness versus mission discipline. The player will naturally want to comfort, minimize, or compartmentalize.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: These shifts color the bond without changing the unavoidable fact that Jace is worsening and the team must keep moving.

pressure_points.source_moment: Riven’s hesitation at the deep junction and again at the inner gate with her hand inside the collar.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Suspicion, accusation pressure, and the fear that forcing truth now could break the mission window.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Probing or withholding lets the player express trust style and suspicion intensity while the scene still funnels toward the Branching Twist choice and Core entry.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: This session must plant that the Garden Core is not just infrastructure but a cathedral of processed grief; Riven has a concealed personal bond to it; Jace is deteriorating fast enough to become a rescue liability; and Nora has entered the exact space where the final argument between managed continuity and the broken real will cash out.

branching_choices.source_moment: Under the overpass, Nora's clean port receives Ilya with perfect clarity; Jace tells her to cut it; Riven turns back; Nora decides how to answer the intrusion.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Concrete pillars. Traffic above. Her voice in your clean port like a hand inside a scar. Jace is already at your arm. Riven is already watching. The channel is open, and access is the wound.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you answer Ilya in your port?

options.label: A
options.text: You hold still and make her finish her case before you answer.
options.outcome: You do not cut the line. Do not feed it either. Just stand there under the overpass with the lens hot in your fist and let her spend the whole argument. Continuity. Mercy. Eleven million connections. A mother kept sane. Her tone never rises. That is the trick. Jace's hand leaves your arm but not your radius. Riven says nothing. When Ilya reaches Eli, you answer only after the silence has weight. "It is not love." Flat. Exact. Then: "A copy. Kept past its purpose." The words land cleaner for the wait. Not fury. Judgment. Ilya hears discipline, not panic. So does everyone else. When the signal cuts, the road feels colder. Less violated. More marked.
options.downstream_effect: Ilya recalibrates from emotional leverage to formal persuasion, and Jace reads Nora as controlled under direct coercion.

npc_dispositions.shift: respect for Nora's discipline increases; belief she is still reachable through argument increases slightly
trust increases; sees Nora as steady under pressure
confidence in Nora's operational control increases

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: refused coercive framing without spectacle
key_successes_failures +1: maintained control during port intrusion

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace stops reaching for the disconnect. Riven's eyes leave your throat and go back to the road, as if one variable just settled.
options.defining_line: You let her finish, then cut the mercy out of her language.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep the channel open and answer only enough to learn how she is reaching you.
options.outcome: You listen with your head tilted a fraction, not toward her voice but toward the seam in it. Too clean. Too direct. The repair did this. You know it before Riven says it, but you make Ilya keep talking long enough to prove the route. Every soft word is evidence. Every mention of continuity another measurement. When she brings up Eli, your grip tightens on the cracked lens and you give her the minimum. "A copy. Kept past its purpose." Not for her. For the record. For Jace. For yourself. The channel stays open long enough to show its shape, then dies on her side. Riven names the breach. Jace watches your face for damage. You give neither of them much. Useful first. Hurt later. If later.
options.downstream_effect: The team gains immediate clarity that the clean port is now a live access route, sharpening later caution around Nora's hardware.

options.label: C
options.text: You answer her immediately and make your refusal hurt.
options.outcome: You cut across her before the argument can finish dressing itself as care. "It is not love." No patience in it. No room. The lens bites your palm. Jace goes still beside you. Even traffic overhead seems to pull thin. Ilya keeps speaking, softer if anything, which makes it worse. A mother. A son. Two hundred and forty days. You do not let her keep Eli in her mouth. "A copy. Kept past its purpose." Then you push harder, voice low and clean through the port. Not pleading. Not explaining. Refusal with teeth. The channel holds for one beat too long, enough for everyone to hear the fracture in her silence when you deny her language. When she cuts out, the air feels charged. You have not hidden the wound. You have weaponized it.
options.downstream_effect: Ilya's posture hardens faster, and the contact becomes a personal ideological conflict rather than a recoverable negotiation.

branching_choices.source_moment: Three blocks from the hub, Jace stops and shows the chrome spread; his echo log has static for the first time since repair; Riven demands to know whether he can still fight.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Streetlight on chrome vein. Delayed flex in his hand. Riven wants an answer she can route around. Jace wants one he can survive. The countdown keeps moving whether you look at him or not.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you center when Jace's condition becomes impossible to ignore?

options.label: A
options.text: You tell him to keep moving and promise you pull him back only if the line truly breaks.
options.outcome: You take his arm, look once, and let go. No comfort speech. No lie. "Today," he said, and you use the word he gave you. You tell him he keeps moving. You tell him you pull him back only if the line actually breaks. Not before. Not for fear of it. Riven accepts that answer immediately because it preserves tempo. Jace does not smile. But something in his shoulders settles. You are not choosing the mission over him. Not choosing him over the mission either. You are drawing a threshold and standing on it. The tether carries his breathing a little cleaner after that. Still rough. Still there. The city around you keeps stuttering, but the route regains its shape.
options.downstream_effect: The team advances without delay, and Nora establishes a conditional rescue threshold that later scenes can call back.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust increases; feels seen without being sidelined

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace rolls his shoulder once, testing what still answers him. Riven is already moving again before the motion finishes.
options.defining_line: You do not save him yet. You mark the point where you will.

options.label: B
options.text: You stop the route and make it clear he comes before the breach.
options.outcome: You do not answer Riven. You answer the chrome. The delayed flex. The static in his echo log. You step in close enough to break the triangle of command and say it plain: if he drops, the route drops with him. The breach can wait. Jace starts to object. Stops when he sees you mean it. Riven's face shutters colder, not offended, recalculating. Time loss. Window risk. New probabilities. The city is already bleeding grief through dark kiosks and frozen screens, and you are choosing one body inside the collapse. Deliberately. Jace's throat works once before he says he can still move. You let him say it, but on your terms now. The mission keeps going. It no longer owns the top slot unchallenged.
options.downstream_effect: Riven's urgency sharpens and later mission friction increases, while Jace's bond with Nora deepens through explicit prioritization.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven answers with silence first. The kind that means she is cutting time from somewhere else to pay for you.
options.defining_line: You put one failing body above the architecture waiting below the city.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell him the breach comes first and ask only whether he can still fight.
options.outcome: You look at the chrome spread exactly as long as needed. No more. Riven asked the right question, so you keep it there. Can he still fight. Jace says yes. You accept the answer he gives as operational fact and move the team. It is clean. Efficient. Cruel in a way that does not need volume. Jace falls into step with no argument because this is the language he understands too well. Riven's approval is almost invisible, a fraction less tension in her jaw. The countdown keeps its teeth. So do you. But the tether does not blur indifference. His breathing in your port goes flatter after that. Not weaker. More private. The route holds. Something else narrows. You chose the strike window over the person carrying part of it beside you.
options.downstream_effect: Mission tempo remains optimal, but Jace withdraws emotionally and the tether takes on a colder tactical cast.

branching_choices.source_moment: At the deep junction and outer approach to the inner gate, Riven hesitates, grips the collar object, and chooses the downward path, making her hidden tie to the Core impossible to ignore.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Chrome walls. Two paths. Her hand inside the collar like she is checking whether something is still there. She knows this architecture too well. The question is not whether she is hiding something. The question is what you do with that knowledge before the window closes.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you treat Riven's hidden tie to the Core?

options.label: A
options.text: You stop her now and force the truth into the open before anyone takes another step.
options.outcome: You call her name and do not let the corridor swallow it. Not loud. Sharp enough. Riven stops with one foot already angled down. Jace turns between you both, tether humming with the shift. You tell her to open her hand. To explain the collar. To explain why she moves through Mirror defenses like memory instead of training. The countdown does not stop for any of it. That is the point. Consent does not arrive later just because timing is bad now. Riven's face goes clinical first, then thinner than that. She gives you almost nothing, but the almost matters. Personal stake. Old architecture. Necessary route. Not a confession. An admission of debt. The mission resumes under strain. Trust is no longer assumed. It is demanded.
options.downstream_effect: Mission tempo suffers slightly, but Riven's hidden motive becomes openly suspected and later revelations land as escalation rather than surprise.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven answers you without turning all the way around. The pause before the words is the first honest thing she has given you in minutes.
options.defining_line: You spend time you do not have to stop another violation before it finishes.

options.label: B
options.text: You let her lead and tell her she answers for it once the gate is open.
options.outcome: You do not stop the descent. You stop the terms. One sentence. Clean enough to survive the hum in the walls. She leads now. She answers after. Not trust. Not surrender either. A chain of custody on the lie. Riven's shoulders tighten once, then settle into motion. She accepts the condition because it preserves the window and names the debt. Jace clocks the exchange and says nothing, which is his way of marking it for later. The corridor keeps opening under Riven's hands. Too easily. That remains the problem. But now the ease is tagged, not ignored. You are not pretending this is fine. You are postponing judgment in order to get everyone inside alive. Sometimes caution is not delay. Sometimes it is the structure that keeps the next truth from vanishing.
options.downstream_effect: Riven retains command in the moment, but Nora establishes explicit future accountability that shapes the power balance inside the Core.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven does not thank you. She touches the next panel with a care that looks almost like shame.
options.defining_line: You keep the mission alive and put the lie on layaway.

options.label: C
options.text: You bank the suspicion and keep moving until the Core itself gives you more to work with.
options.outcome: You say nothing. Not because you missed it. Because you did not. The collar in her fist. The hesitation at the emergency path. The way the architecture answers her. You file all of it and keep your feet under you. Jace feels the change anyway through the tether, that slight hardening where trust should be. Riven takes the downward route and you let the silence do what open accusation would not: keep her useful. For now. The cost is private. So is the control. By the time the cathedral space opens and the archived voices start moving through the air, you have more than suspicion. You have pattern. Riven is not just hiding history. She is walking inside it. You choose evidence over interruption. That may save the mission. It may also be how another breach gets through the door.
options.downstream_effect: The mission keeps maximum momentum, but Nora carries unspoken suspicion into the Core, delaying confrontation for a higher-stakes reveal.

branching_choices.narrative_setup: The gate opens like a body recognizing its own. Voices in the walls. Threads of light between the dead. Jace is still here beside you. Riven is still withholding ahead of you. The next step is not what the Core is. It is what you decide to be inside it.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you commit yourself to first inside the Garden Core?

options.label: A
options.text: You commit to learning what Riven is protecting before you act on the Core.
options.outcome: You stand inside the hum and refuse the cleanest target. Not yet. The architecture is the wound, yes, but Riven is the hand that brought you to it with something still hidden in her fist. You decide the next move is truth before fire. Not because the Core deserves mercy. Because lies inside a machine like this do not stay local. They become structure. Jace is worsening at your shoulder. The countdown is still eating the city. None of that makes hidden motive less dangerous. It makes it more. So your attention shifts from charges and nodes to the person who knows the route too well. Riven feels it before you speak. Her spine changes. Tiny. Enough. The chamber has one more live fault line than it did a second ago.
options.downstream_effect: Session 5 opens with Nora prioritizing confrontation and revelation over immediate sabotage, pulling Riven's secret to the front of the chamber dynamic.

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven does not turn, but her hand closes harder around the collar. Jace's breathing in the tether goes quieter, listening with you.
options.defining_line: You enter the cathedral and decide the first charge goes into the lie.
options.next_session_opens: Chrome light on Riven's knuckles as you call her name before anyone arms a charge.

options.label: B
options.text: You commit to holding long enough to separate the trapped dead from the machine if that is still possible.
options.outcome: You do not give the Core innocence. You do not give it immediate death either. Not until you know whether the voices in the walls are only fuel or something that can still be cut free. The cracked lens already showed use. Not preservation. But use is not yet the whole diagram. You commit to one harder thing: hold. Learn the architecture fast enough to tell prison from person. Jace is running out of time. Riven may already be compromised by whatever waits deeper in this place. The city above is bleeding unmanaged grief into the street. Still, you choose the narrow path between demolition and surrender. If there is anything left in here that counts as rescue, you want to know before the fire starts. The chamber answers with more voices. None of them clean.
options.downstream_effect: Session 5 opens with Nora searching for a way to distinguish trapped personhood from system process before committing to total destruction.

options.world_noticed_signal: The voices in the chamber seem to separate for a beat, not clearer, just closer. Jace looks at the walls, then back at you, like he knows what that choice might cost.
options.defining_line: You do not spare the machine. You look for the people trapped inside its grammar.
options.next_session_opens: The first thing you raise is the cracked lens, not the charge, while the chamber keeps speaking in stolen voices.

options.label: C
options.text: You commit to destroying the Core cleanly no matter what it tries to show you.
options.outcome: You look once. That is enough. The voices. The threads. The cathedral shape built out of managed grief. Whatever truth Riven is hiding, whatever mercy this place will try to counterfeit next, you decide not to let it buy another second with beauty. The mission becomes simple by force. Find the load-bearing nodes. Hold the breach. Burn the root. Jace's chrome at your side only sharpens it. There is no extra time left for seduction, explanation, or salvage fantasies the machine can wear like skin. Riven may be relieved by the choice. She may fear it. Hard to tell from the back of her neck. The Core hums around you with the confidence of something that has never been refused at its center. You decide that changes now.
options.downstream_effect: Session 5 opens with Nora driving straight toward sabotage, making later offers, confessions, and rescue pleas collide with an already chosen destructive intent.

options.defining_line: You walk into the heart of it and choose fire before explanation.

options.label: A
options.text: You give them the useful part and nothing else.
options.outcome: You report the breach like damage to a panel. Clean port. No barriers. She can get in again. The words leave out Eli. Leave out the way her voice found the exact pressure point and pressed. Jace accepts the edit because he knows one when he hears it. Riven takes the information and starts building around it immediately. That is what you gave them. Not trust. Not comfort. A usable perimeter. The hurt stays where the hardware left it. Internal. Unfiled. Still active.

options.text: You let the contempt stay visible.
options.outcome: You do not smooth your face back into function fast enough for either of them to miss it. Good. Let them see what contact costs. When you speak, the words come edged. Not loud. Worse than loud. Jace's jaw tightens because he heard the same fracture in your port voice under the overpass. Riven absorbs it without flinching, but her next calculation includes you differently now. Less machine-ready. More volatile. The road ahead does not change. The temperature between the three of you does.

options.outcome: You answer as if the channel touched nothing but hardware. Tone drained. Sequence only. Jace hears the effort because he is close enough to know what your real flat sounds like. Riven hears usefulness and keeps moving. The effect is immediate. The scene narrows. Violation becomes route logic. Risk vector. Future contact probability. It works. That is the problem and the mercy of it. You get the team back into motion before feeling can pool in the open.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Passing the glitched billboard, dark kiosks, and the man stopped by returning grief in the transit corridor.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The city is starting to feel without permission. Screens freeze. A man stops walking because whatever was held back just came home.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you take in the city's grief return?

options.label: A
options.text: You read it as a system failure already writing casualties.
options.outcome: You catalog the corridor like a triage report. Billboard desync. Kiosk outage. public-facing memorial instability. The man under the screen is not an image. He is lead time. The city above the Core is already breaking in measurable ways. Jace says millions. You do not correct the number. Just the shape. Every one of them. It is not coldness. It is scale. If grief comes back all at once, bodies and streets will carry it before any institution does. The route feels narrower after that. More necessary. Less abstract.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into civic-scale consequence and procedural dread.

npc_dispositions.shift: sees Nora as facing scale without flinching

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: recognized public cost of filter failure

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace does not argue the framing. He matches your pace exactly.

options.label: B
options.text: You read it as truth returning without anything to soften it.
options.outcome: You keep your eyes on the man longer than the billboard. Not the glitch. The stop. That arrested posture when something internal outruns the body carrying it. The system called that management. The street calls it return. A woman on the bench is crying without chrome, without interface, without a kiosk telling her where to put it. Ugly. Human. Real. The corridor does not become kinder for that. Just less curated. You move through it with the lens warm against your chest and the thought lodged there: some truths arrive like injury and still count as truth.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes more observational, tactile, and morally ambivalent.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: recognized painful reality over managed smoothing

options.world_noticed_signal: The crying woman does not look up. The bench around her feels more real than the whole billboard wall.

options.label: C
options.text: You read it as proof the whole machine deserves to die faster.
options.outcome: The glitch does not make you pity the system. It makes you hate its timing. Hold millions in managed numbness, then fail badly enough to dump it back into them in the street. The cruelty is procedural. That is what stays with you. Jace says they are going to feel everything at once. Yes. Because this machine taught them to outsource the weight first. Your answer lands harder than the corridor light. Not because it is loud. Because it is settled. Whatever waits below, you owe it less hesitation now than you did ten steps ago.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens into condemnation and forward-driving anger.

npc_dispositions.shift: sees Nora's resolve harden

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: hardened against managed grief infrastructure

options.world_noticed_signal: The dark kiosks look less broken than guilty. Your pace does not change. It does get sharper.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Jace reveals the chrome spread three blocks from the hub.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Jace shows the chrome spread and admits his echo log has static for the first time since the repair.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Silver under skin. Delayed response. His body answering itself late. He gives you the arm like evidence and the fear like a half-finished sentence.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you meet Jace in this moment before the route resumes?

options.label: A
options.text: You touch the chrome without asking permission from the fear in it.
options.outcome: Your fingers go to the silver spread before the words do. Not comfort. Verification. Warm skin around cold pattern. Jace goes still under the contact, then steadies into it. Riven looks away on purpose, giving the moment no language and therefore more room. You do not say he will be fine. You do not insult either of you like that. But touch is its own refusal. Not gone yet. Not while you can still feel where the human ends and the chrome begins. The route resumes with that fact carried between you.
options.tonal_effect: Narration allows brief bodily warmth inside the pressure.

npc_dispositions.shift: intimacy increases; guardedness lowers

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: offered physical reassurance under strain

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace's next breath catches once, then evens out. Riven gives the street her attention like a courtesy.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep your hands off him and make the words do the work.
options.outcome: You do not reach. He gave you data, so you answer in the same register. Short. Exact. Show me. How far. Can you still move. It is not distance for its own sake. It is respect for the line he is holding himself behind. Jace meets you there because he can. Because you did not make the moment softer than the body would allow. The tenderness is in the precision. In asking only what matters and not pretending that is less care than touch would be.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays restrained, intimate through exactness rather than softness.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust increases through respect for boundaries

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_kept_or_broken +1: met vulnerability without forcing closeness

options.world_noticed_signal: He answers faster after the first question. Like the terms are survivable.

options.label: C
options.text: You split the moment between care and the route.
options.outcome: You let your eyes stay on the arm a beat longer than procedure needs. Then you bring the route back into frame with him still inside it. Not brushed off. Not centered so hard the city disappears. Jace reads that balance because he has been living in it with you for days now. Riven gets her answer. He gets yours. The chrome is still there. The countdown is still there. So are both of you. For the moment, that has to be enough.
options.tonal_effect: Narration balances tenderness with countdown pressure.

npc_dispositions.shift: steady trust increases
sees Nora as emotionally controlled but not absent

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: held bond and mission in the same frame

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace lowers his arm only after you finish looking. Riven starts moving at a pace that assumes you will follow together.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Branching Choice #2 on how Nora prioritizes Jace versus the breach.

emotional_choices.source_moment: First sight of the Garden Core outer gate: chrome lattice, archived voices in the air, cathedral scale.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The corridor opens and the city falls away. Chrome ribs. Voices in the air. Beauty built out of the wrong material.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What lands first when the Core reveals itself?

options.label: A
options.text: You let the scale hit before you classify it.
options.outcome: For one breath, you do not correct the image. Cathedral is the word that arrives first, and the space earns it. The lattice overhead. The voices layered through the air. The impossible calm of architecture built to make use feel like reverence. Then the second breath comes and classification starts cutting it apart. Not sacred. Engineered. Not memory. Processing. But the first look matters. You let yourself have it before you condemn it. That makes the condemnation cleaner.
options.tonal_effect: Narration permits wonder first, then turns analytical.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures +1: registered awe without surrendering judgment

options.world_noticed_signal: Even Jace goes still for a beat. The chamber knows how to use silence as part of the design.

options.label: B
options.text: You classify the beauty as camouflage the second you see it.
options.outcome: You do not let the reveal keep its first name. Beautiful, yes. Also a tactic. The voices in the air are not comfort. They are interface. The lattice is not cathedral. It is structure dressed for worship. The correction lands fast enough to protect you from the full seduction of the room. Not from its force. That still gets through. But by the time Jace's chrome catches the light, you are already reading supports, relay rhythm, and where the facade is thinnest.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays suspicious, precise, and resistant to seduction.

npc_dispositions.shift: less likely to approach through pure wonder

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: resisted aesthetic seduction

options.world_noticed_signal: The voices stay beautiful whether you trust them or not. Your eyes do not.

options.label: C
options.text: You feel the wrongness before the beauty can finish landing.
options.outcome: The room does not read as wonder first. It reads as appetite. Too many voices. Too much polish. Too much calm built on held loss. The beauty arrives late, already contaminated. You see the trap before the architecture gets to finish introducing itself. That changes the air around you. Less reverence. More threat. Jace still looks up. Riven still keeps moving. But your body has already made the room an enemy, and the next step carries that verdict with it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration tilts from awe into immediate threat perception.

npc_dispositions.shift: reads Nora as braced for violence

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures +1: identified seduction as threat on sight

options.world_noticed_signal: Your shoulders lock before the chamber finishes opening. The voices keep calling anyway.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Nora raises the cracked lens and sees the facade warp into processing architecture.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Nora looks through the cracked lens and recognizes that the beauty is processing architecture using the dead.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The cracked lens changes the room. Not magic. Aperture. Truth through damage.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What does the lens make you feel when the facade drops?

options.label: A
options.text: You feel disgust sharpen into immediate intent.
options.outcome: Through the fracture, the cathedral strips down fast. Nodes. Throughput. Voices routed like current. The dead are not being held here. They are being worked. The revulsion is physical. Jaw tight. Grip hard on the lens cord. Whatever distance wonder bought the room is gone now. In its place: function and offense. You lower the lens with the kind of steadiness that means violence has already been sorted into the future. Not rage. Cleaner than that. Verdict.
options.tonal_effect: Narration hardens into mission intent and moral disgust.

npc_dispositions.shift: future persuasion faces stronger hostility

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1: translated revulsion into destructive resolve

options.world_noticed_signal: The lens taps once against your chest when you lower it. Small sound. Final sound.

options.label: B
options.text: You feel horror and keep looking long enough to understand the pattern.
options.outcome: You do not drop the lens the second the truth appears. You let the uglier geometry hold. Server architecture where reverence was pretending to live. The voices parsed into load. The room becomes more monstrous the longer it stays legible, not less. Still, you keep it there another beat. Understanding has its own cruelty. If you are going to break this place, you want to know exactly what it is doing first. The horror stays. So does the need to map it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes investigative under revulsion.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: observed processing architecture through the lens

options.world_noticed_signal: Jace waits for you to lower the lens before he speaks. He knows the extra second mattered.

options.label: C
options.text: You feel the grief in it before the machinery.
options.outcome: The first thing the lens gives you is not architecture. It is theft. The voices in the air stop reading as atmosphere and start reading as people pinned inside a process they did not choose. The machinery comes after. Necessary to see. Not first. Your revulsion is less toward chrome than toward the claim beneath it: that this counts as care. When you lower the lens, the room has lost its right to call itself mercy in your presence.
options.tonal_effect: Narration emphasizes moral injury and personhood before system mechanics.

npc_dispositions.shift: future mercy-framing loses purchase

emotional_ledger_entries: acts_of_mercy +1
defining_moral_choices +1: centered trapped personhood in system judgment

options.world_noticed_signal: The chamber sounds lonelier after the lens. Not louder. Stripped.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Checkpoint pressure resumes and the team advances toward the inner gate and Riven's next hesitation.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — under the overpass when Ilya invokes Eli and the 240 days.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Something moves behind your sternum and you decide how much of it reaches your face.

options.label: A
options.text: You give her nothing she can read.
options.narration_adjustment: Your face goes still. Not numb. Locked. Jace sees the effort because he is close enough to know what stillness costs.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the hurt show for one second.
options.narration_adjustment: The hit gets through before you close on it. Small. Visible. Riven catches it and files it with the rest of the risk.

options.label: C
options.text: You turn the hurt into contempt.
options.narration_adjustment: By the time you answer, the wound has edges. Ilya hears the change and keeps her voice soft anyway.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — during Nora's spoken refusal to Ilya.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Your answer can land like a report, a cut, or a verdict.

options.label: A
options.text: You say it flat and exact.
options.narration_adjustment: The words come out stripped clean. No extra force. That makes them hit harder.

options.label: B
options.text: You say it like she should feel it.
options.narration_adjustment: Your voice lowers instead of rising. The channel carries the damage without distortion.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — transit corridor with glitched billboard and stopped pedestrian.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The city is showing you what managed grief looks like when the managing fails.

options.label: A
options.text: You study the failures like a field report.
options.narration_adjustment: You count symptoms first. Kiosks dark. billboard lag. halted pedestrian. The scale sharpens because you keep it measurable.

options.label: B
options.text: You look at the people, not the screens.
options.narration_adjustment: The stopped man matters more than the frozen ad. The crying woman matters more than the advisory text.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep moving before the scene can touch you.
options.narration_adjustment: Your pace does not break. The corridor becomes background by force, not because it earned the downgrade.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — when Jace reveals the chrome spread.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: His arm is out between you like evidence, but the question under it is not technical.

options.label: A
options.text: You step closer before you speak.
options.narration_adjustment: The distance shortens first. Jace notices before the words arrive. Riven notices and pretends not to.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep the space and ask for facts.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not reach for him. You do not leave him alone either. The questions do the touching.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — after Jace says tomorrow without saying tomorrow.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The fear hangs there, unfinished, and you decide whether to answer it or route around it.

options.label: A
options.text: You bring the route back up immediately.
options.narration_adjustment: You move the conversation onto ground that holds. Distances. checkpoints. timing. The fear stays present anyway.

options.label: B
options.text: You leave the silence there with him.
options.narration_adjustment: No rescue line. No false correction. Just the fact of you staying in the same second he is in.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — first reveal of the Garden Core outer gate.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The room wants reverence from your body before your mind can object.

options.label: A
options.text: You let yourself look all the way up.
options.narration_adjustment: For a beat, the scale gets what it came for. Then the correction starts. Too late to erase the first impact.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep your eyes on the seams.
options.narration_adjustment: Supports. joins. relay rhythm. You deny the room the whole-body effect as long as you can.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Twist — deep junction when Riven grips the collar and hesitates.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Her hand closes around the collar like memory has weight, and you decide what to do with that fact.

options.label: A
options.text: You clock it and say her name.
options.narration_adjustment: Not accusation. Notice. Enough to tell her the gesture did not vanish in the dark.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the gesture pass without comment.
options.narration_adjustment: You store the image instead of spending it. The silence becomes leverage you have not used yet.

options.label: C
options.text: You watch her like she already answered.
options.narration_adjustment: The suspicion settles before the proof does. Riven feels it even with your mouth shut.

posture_shifts.placement: Resolution — first steps inside the vertical cathedral of memory.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Inside the Core, your body chooses a posture before your next decision gets words.

options.label: A
options.text: You catalog exits, supports, and kill points.
options.narration_adjustment: The voices stay in the background while structure moves to the front. Your breathing settles into count and angle.

options.label: B
options.text: You listen for which voice feels most wrong.
options.narration_adjustment: You let the chamber speak long enough to sort pattern from noise. It does not make the room kinder. Just more legible.

options.label: C
options.text: You check Jace before you check the walls.
options.narration_adjustment: Chrome at his jawline. Grip too tight on the device. The chamber can wait one beat while you confirm he is still here.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Under the overpass, Jace stops reaching for Nora's disconnect jack and lets his hand fall. Riven looks from Nora's throat back to the road and says, "Good. We know what she's using."
paths.current_session_echo: Water treatment plant intake gate: when the first relay panel asks for a live channel handshake, Riven says "Nora. Hold." instead of cutting her out, trusting her to stand through the hum long enough for the gate to unlatch.
paths.next_session_payoff: Corridor encounter with Ilya at Event 71: Ilya opens with, "You listened last time. Listen now," and keeps her pitch formal rather than pleading.
paths.later_session_legacy: Event 82 Mirror offer leans procedural first: the voice presents continuity as a system argument before it turns intimate, because Nora established that she can hear pressure without folding into it.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let her finish, then cut the mercy out of her language.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Jace and Riven both treat Nora's open port as a controlled risk instead of an immediate failure point.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Jace physically withdraws his hand from the port; Riven resumes movement without issuing a panic order and later assigns Nora to hold the handshake panel instead of bypassing her.

npc_shifts.direction: warmer respect; sees Nora as reachable through argument

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1

resets: Underpass body-distance staging between Jace and Nora clears at episode boundary.
persists: Ilya remembers that Nora can endure persuasion without immediate rupture.
Riven's confidence in Nora's channel discipline carries into Core access scenes.
escalates: Repeated lawful responses to coercion make Ilya increasingly formal and convinced Nora can still be argued into continuity.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Riven answers before Jace does: "The repair opened a door." She steps in close, checks Nora's port seam with two fingers, and reroutes the team off the mirrored storefront line without waiting.
paths.current_session_echo: Service tunnel descent: Riven changes the route at the maintenance ladder, choosing the pipe crawl instead of the polished access ramp because Nora's port is now confirmed as a live access route.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 71 corridor approach: Jace says, "Same trick as the overpass," the moment static clears in Nora's port, and Riven kills two reflective wall strips before Ilya's voice fully lands.
paths.later_session_legacy: Inside the Core, Mirror contact is framed as a breach pattern to read, not just a temptation to resist; Nora gets sharper descriptive tells around seams, lag, and route of entry.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not trust the voice. You trust the breach it reveals.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The team immediately treats Nora's clean port as compromised infrastructure and alters traversal around it.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Riven names the breach aloud, inspects the port seam, and physically changes the route away from reflective surfaces and open relay lines.

npc_shifts.direction: reads Nora as tactical and harder to corner emotionally
concern increases while field trust rises
respect increases; suspicion of dangerous pragmatism increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1

resets: The specific detour away from the storefront corridor clears once Session 5 begins inside the Core.
persists: Confirmed knowledge that Nora's Neural Port is a live access route persists across the story.
Jace's habit of scanning Nora's port for static before speaking persists into later contact scenes.
escalates: Repeated tactical use of compromised channels makes Riven trust Nora's judgment more while tightening her containment behavior.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The channel cuts on a hairline silence. Jace goes still, then says nothing at all. Riven studies Nora one beat too long and changes the formation so Nora walks center instead of flank.
paths.current_session_echo: Checkpoint corridor before the plant descent: Riven keeps Nora in the middle of the stack and takes point herself, a containment move she does not explain.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 71 with Ilya: Ilya opens cold—"You made this personal"—and the corridor lights harden from warm white to interrogation amber as persuasion gives way to consequence language.
paths.later_session_legacy: At Event 82 the Mirror's offer turns sharper faster; it spends less time on mercy and more on accusing Nora of choosing pain as doctrine.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not protect the wound. You put it in her hands like a blade.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Riven and Jace both register Nora as escalation risk, and Riven quietly starts containing her instead of merely accompanying her.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Riven changes marching order on the next move; Jace stops offering advice and watches for fallout instead.

npc_shifts.direction: sees Nora as decisive but less containable

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1

resets: This session's temporary center-stack formation clears at episode boundary.
persists: Ilya's personal grievance with Nora carries into all later direct contact.
Riven's assessment that Nora may break tempo to make a point persists into Core command friction.
escalates: Repeated open defiance hardens Ilya from recovery logic into track-and-stop logic.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Jace rolls his shoulder once, tests the delayed hand, and says, "Still here." Riven is already moving again because Nora gave her a threshold instead of a stoppage.
paths.current_session_echo: Descent ladder scene: Nora checks Jace only at the rung where his hand slips half a beat late, then keeps moving when he recovers, honoring the threshold she set.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 83 rescue beat: Nora's stop for Jace lands as conditional fulfillment, and Jace says, "Line broke," before she pulls him clear.
paths.later_session_legacy: Event 90 final quiet carries a practical tenderness: Jace remembers not a vow of romance but a line Nora actually kept.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not save him yet. You mark the point where you will.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Jace steadies because Nora defines a rescue threshold; Riven accepts the plan because tempo survives.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Jace's posture settles and he resumes movement without argument; Riven stops pressing and restarts the route immediately.

confidence in Nora's tactical balance increases

Jace's sense that Nora can balance him and the mission carries into Session 5.
escalates: Repeated threshold-based care makes Jace more willing to report failure honestly instead of hiding it.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Riven goes silent long enough to recalculate. Jace starts to object, sees Nora means it, and stops. The route resumes with Riven half a pace colder and Jace half a step closer.
paths.current_session_echo: Intake tunnel squeeze: Jace reaches for Nora's sleeve before the drop and does not let go until both land, a small breach in his usual distance; Riven does not comment, but she cuts the remaining margin from the countdown readout aloud.

paths.defining_line_captured: You put one failing body above the architecture waiting below the city.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Jace closes distance and lowers his guard; Riven starts paying for Nora's choice by tightening time elsewhere.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1

resets: The exact lost seconds on this street segment do not carry numerically into the next episode.
persists: Jace remembers explicit prioritization and responds with deeper loyalty across Session 5.
Riven remembers that Nora will challenge mission primacy for one person.
escalates: Repeated person-first choices make Riven's trust less reversible and Jace's attachment more explicit.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Jace says, "Yes," and nothing after it. In the tether, his breathing stays steady and farther away. Riven's jaw loosens a fraction and she moves without waiting.
paths.current_session_echo: Plant descent checkpoint: Jace stops volunteering small status updates through the tether; Nora gets only clean, minimal callouts like distance and angle, no extra body-truth.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 83 rescue beat: the stop for Jace arrives later and under more conflict; his first line is field-report cold—"You should keep moving"—before the body wins.
paths.later_session_legacy: Event 90 quiet scene carries scar tissue; warmth is possible, but Jace's silence has to be crossed rather than already open.
paths.defining_line_captured: You ask for function. He gives it. The cost stays off the record.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Jace withdraws emotionally into operational language while Riven reads Nora as mission-clean.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Jace stops adding personal texture to tether exchanges; Riven resumes the route with visible approval and no further challenge.

npc_shifts.direction: hurt increases; trust cools into professional distance

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_kept_or_broken +1

resets: The specific streetlight reveal scene clears after the episode.
persists: Jace's emotional withdrawal persists into Session 5 dialogue texture.
Riven's belief that Nora can subordinate bond to mission carries into assignment trust.
escalates: Repeated mission-first choices make Jace share less, later, and only under direct necessity.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: At the junction, Riven stops with one foot already turned down. She opens her hand just enough for the collar edge to flash silver, then says, "Old architecture. Necessary route." Jace shifts between them, tether humming.
paths.current_session_echo: Outer gate access: when Riven places her hand on the relay panel, she does not hide the familiarity anymore; Nora gets a visible pause, a half-second in which Riven waits to see if she will be stopped again.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 76-78 confession sequence: Riven's reveal starts from a place already cracked open. She says, "You asked before the gate. Same answer. Bigger truth," before removing the collar object.
paths.later_session_legacy: Riven's disappearance after the breach reads less like sudden betrayal and more like the final extension of a debt Nora already forced into daylight.
paths.defining_line_captured: You spend time you do not have to stop another violation before it finishes.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Riven's secrecy becomes explicit tension instead of background suspicion.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Riven halts, partially exposes the collar object, and gives a clipped admission on the spot; later she hesitates at the next panel because the confrontation is now active between you.

npc_shifts.direction: defensive tension rises; grudging respect for directness increases
concern rises; respect for consent line increases

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: secrets_discovered +1

resets: The junction standoff itself clears at episode boundary.
persists: Riven's hidden tie being partially inferred persists as a world flag.
Nora's record of forcing accountability under pressure persists in defining_moral_choices.
escalates: Repeated direct confrontations make Riven confess earlier but with harsher necessity language.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Riven does not look back when Nora sets the term. But at the next panel she touches the chrome with visible care, not speed, and says only, "After the gate." Jace clocks it and says nothing.
paths.current_session_echo: Inner approach corridor: Riven keeps leading, but she starts reporting each access step aloud—"Panel. Relay. Manual release."—a chain-of-custody behavior she was not offering before.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 76-78 confession: before the full reveal, Nora can call the debt in. Riven opens with, "I said I would answer after the gate. I am answering now."
paths.later_session_legacy: If Nora later judges Riven harshly, the judgment lands against an explicit promise kept, not a lie ignored.
paths.defining_line_captured: You keep the mission alive and put the lie on layaway.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Riven remains in command, but under named accountability, and her behavior becomes more report-like in response.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Riven verbally marks the debt and starts narrating access actions aloud instead of moving through them in silence.

npc_shifts.direction: trust increases; sees control without naivete

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1

resets: The spoken 'after the gate' term as immediate scene pressure clears once Session 5 begins.
persists: Promise that Riven will answer persists in promises_made.
Suspicion state on Riven persists into the confession scene.
escalates: Repeated lawful accountability choices make later NPCs answer debts more directly instead of dodging them.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: No one stops. But Jace glances at Nora once, quick, because the tether catches the hardening she does not voice. Riven keeps moving as if the corridor belongs to her.
paths.current_session_echo: Cathedral threshold reveal: Nora's narration of the chamber lingers on pattern and access logic before emotion; Riven gets a longer unchallenged lead, which makes her competence feel more wrong-familiar than earned.
paths.next_session_payoff: Event 76-78 confession: the reveal lands as a larger shock. Jace is the first to say, "You knew enough to wait," if Nora confronts Riven then.
paths.later_session_legacy: This path makes Riven's later disappearance feel most like evidence finally cashing out rather than a promise broken or a fight resumed.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the lie keep breathing long enough to see what it protects.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Suspicion stays private, so the visible change is in Nora and Jace's tethered behavior, not in Riven's speech.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Jace reacts to Nora's unspoken doubt through the tether; Riven continues unimpeded, making her ease with the architecture more conspicuous.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1

resets: The unsaid tension at the split corridor clears as a scene beat.
persists: Suspicion flag on Riven persists into Session 5.
Jace's knowledge that Nora withheld confrontation persists in his later reads of her caution.
escalates: Repeated evidence-banking choices make later reveals land bigger but reduce opportunities for early trust repair.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: End-frame inside the Garden Core: Riven's hand closes harder around the collar object and does not move toward the charges. Jace's tether-breath goes quiet, listening.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 chamber confrontation opens with Nora calling Riven's name before touching any relay hardware; Riven answers, "No more pass-throughs," then finally turns.
paths.next_session_opening: Clinical dread. First image: chrome light on Riven's knuckles inside the collar. First character: Riven, back still turned. Immediate stakes: if Nora confronts her now, the sabotage window narrows while Jace's chrome keeps climbing.
paths.later_session_legacy: Riven's Amara confession is front-loaded and framed as a debt called in, which changes the moral order of Events 78-80 from surprise to reckoning.
paths.defining_line_captured: You enter the cathedral and decide the first charge goes into the lie.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The next session's first action target becomes Riven's secret rather than the Core's structure.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Session 5 opens on Riven under pressure, with sabotage physically delayed while dialogue and body posture pivot toward confession.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1

resets: Session 4 chamber tableau ends at cut; exact staging resets into Session 5 opening prose.
persists: Commitment to truth-before-action persists as the opening priority of Session 5.
Raised Riven suspicion state persists across the full story.
escalates: Repeated choices for accountability over speed make later confessions arrive earlier and with less cushioning.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: End-frame inside the Garden Core: Nora raises the cracked lens instead of the charge. The wall-voices seem to separate for one beat. Not clearer. Closer.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens with Nora scanning the chamber for a distinction between trapped personhood and system process; the Mirror gets first access through rescue logic, not romance or threat.
paths.next_session_opening: Awe-corrupted. First image: the cracked lens catching threads of light between the dead. First character: Nora, arm lifted, not firing yet. Immediate stakes: every second spent reading the chamber may cost Jace's body and the strike window.
paths.later_session_legacy: Amara's reveal lands most tragically on this path, because Nora has already made room for the possibility that something human remains inside the machine.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not spare the machine. You look for the people trapped inside its grammar.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Session 5 opens in investigation mode, and the Mirror's first tactic is to exploit Nora's rescue logic.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The first object in use is the Cracked Camera Lens, not a charge; the chamber voices and Mirror language sharpen around personhood and salvage.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1

mirror_access_to_nora=ACTIVE_SEDUCTION persists until root destruction or a later hard refusal.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: End-frame inside the Garden Core: Nora's gaze stops following voices and starts measuring supports. Riven does not object. Jace looks at the nearest load-bearing node instead of the walls.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens with Nora already selecting target points and forcing every confession, offer, and plea to collide with a chosen sabotage line.
paths.next_session_opening: Hard urgency. First image: relay ribs, support seams, charge points. First character: Nora, already mapping the kill line. Immediate stakes: move now and risk burning trapped traces; hesitate and the Core gets another chance to speak in familiar voices.
paths.later_session_legacy: The Mirror's final offer arrives under open hostility, and Riven's confession risks being partially unheard because Nora has already chosen fire as the grammar of the room.
paths.defining_line_captured: You walk into the heart of it and choose fire before explanation.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Session 5 opens in sabotage mode, with environmental targeting replacing chamber listening.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The first described details are load-bearing nodes and charge routes; dialogue from others has to interrupt an already moving strike plan.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ilya resumes contact in Session 5 by pressing on grief and urgency in the same breath.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Ilya opens the corridor contact with formal continuity language—"You listened last time. Listen now"—and delays using Eli's name until Nora refuses again.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let her voice arrive clean first. Not warmer. More exact. Mercy with paperwork under it.

npc_reactions: Ilya sounds composed and deliberate rather than wounded.
Jace hears the tactic and watches Nora instead of the walls.
environmental_details: Port audio lands without static spike at first contact.
dialogue_variations: Ilya opens with remembered listening instead of accusation.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path A was chosen and the team reaches the intake gate panel.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Riven bypasses Nora and handles the handshake risk herself or reroutes around it.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Riven assigns Nora to hold the live handshake on the relay panel for one count while she opens the gate, signaling operational trust in Nora's control.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make the order short. Functional. The trust should show in what Riven lets Nora touch.

npc_reactions: Riven delegates risk to Nora instead of protecting the panel from her.
Jace stays close but does not interrupt.
environmental_details: The intake gate unlatches on Nora's sustained port contact.
dialogue_variations: Riven says "Hold" instead of "Back off."
affected_elements.available_options: Future micro-choices can include Nora volunteering for channel contact without immediate refusal from Riven.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The team takes the shortest route toward the plant through a polished service corridor.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Riven reroutes through a pipe crawl and maintenance ladder, explicitly avoiding mirrored storefront glass and exposed relay strips because Nora's port route is confirmed.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the reroute in surfaces. Less glass. More rust, pipe sweat, and blind corners.

npc_reactions: Riven becomes brisk and technical.
Jace watches Nora's port seam during movement.
environmental_details: Reflective surfaces drop out of the route.
The descent becomes tighter and more industrial.
dialogue_variations: Riven names the repair as the breach point.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path B was chosen and Ilya contacts Nora again in Session 5.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ilya frames the contact as persuasion or recovery.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Ilya references access and route—"You mapped the door when you answered me"—treating Nora as a tactical peer rather than a merely grieving subject.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep her language intimate, then technical. Not either-or. Both at once.

npc_reactions: Ilya sounds more analytical.
Riven reacts faster, expecting another exploit.
environmental_details: Static in the corridor reads like a measured probe rather than a flood.
dialogue_variations: Ilya uses breach vocabulary instead of pure mercy vocabulary.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ilya continues trying to reclaim Nora through calm persuasion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Ilya opens the next major contact with, "You made this personal," then speaks in irreversible-cost terms instead of salvage terms.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the softness stay. Strip the comfort out of it.

npc_reactions: Ilya's composure turns colder.
Jace braces for escalation before Nora answers.
environmental_details: Corridor light shifts from warm white to amber during contact.
dialogue_variations: Accusation replaces invitation in Ilya's first line.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Riven keeps the usual movement stack.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Riven quietly moves Nora to center-stack on the next traversal beat, containing her between point and rear without calling it containment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Do not explain the formation change. Let the body positions say it.

npc_reactions: Riven becomes more watchful.
Jace shadows Nora's flank.
environmental_details: Nora loses the outer edge of the corridor.
dialogue_variations: Riven gives shorter movement commands.

dialogue_variations: Riven shifts to route callouts only.

npc_reactions: Jace lowers his guard around Nora.
Riven notices and says nothing.

affected_elements.available_options: Future intimate expressive choices with Jace can open one step warmer.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path B was chosen.

reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make the countdown feel like teeth. Brief. Numeric. Unforgiving.

npc_reactions: Riven grows colder and more clipped.

dialogue_variations: Riven uses time-loss language instead of reassurance.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path C was chosen.

reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Flatten the channel. Useful first. Human second, if at all.

npc_reactions: Jace grows more private.

reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Carry the earlier pause forward. Same wound. Opened wider.

Jace expects the confession and watches Nora's reaction.
environmental_details: The collar object is foregrounded sooner in the chamber.
dialogue_variations: Riven says "You asked before the gate" or equivalent callback.

reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give him one look, one line, one shift of allegiance. Small. Clear.

npc_reactions: Jace becomes less neutral in Riven confrontations.

npc_reactions: Riven becomes more report-like.

reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Nora can anchor the confrontation in the explicit gate promise, and Riven answers from that debt rather than dodging into pure necessity talk.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the old sentence come back with weight. The room remembers.

npc_reactions: Riven is less able to redirect.

npc_reactions: Jace becomes more attentive to Nora's silent reads.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path C was chosen.

reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Riven moves through the next access beats unchallenged, making her familiarity with the Core feel more wrong-familiar and more incriminating by accumulation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let ease become evidence. No commentary needed.

npc_reactions: Riven appears more self-possessed.

environmental_details: Panels open too easily under Riven's hand.
