resets_between_episodes.element: Underpass and corridor body-positioning choices
resets_between_episodes.why: These are scene-specific staging consequences that should be re-authored in the next episode's opening rather than carried as literal coordinates.

resets_between_episodes.element: Exact route detours through maintenance ladders, pipe crawls, and storefront avoidance
resets_between_episodes.why: Those traversal beats belong to Session 4 geography and are no longer actionable once the team is inside the Garden Core.

resets_between_episodes.element: Momentary countdown debt measured in lost seconds from Jace or Riven confrontations
resets_between_episodes.why: The emotional cost persists, but the exact numeric delay is local to the scene and should fold into tone, not remain as a hard timer artifact.

resets_between_episodes.element: Frozen end-frame composition of the Garden Core chamber
resets_between_episodes.why: Session-end tableau exists to hook retention; Session 5 must restage it as active prose rather than preserve a static camera-equivalent state.

persists_across_full_story.why: They define what forms of contact, seduction, or assault the system can use against Nora.
It determines whether Ilya approaches as bureaucrat, persuader, tracker, or grieving witness.
This governs how every later Riven scene reads: manipulator, compromised guide, tragic architect, or all at once.
Jace's bodily decline is a permanent pressure source for rescue urgency, intimacy, and final aftermath tone.
These are the memory spine of Nora's bond with Jace and her accountability with Riven.
They are the story's long-form moral ledger and directly shape Mirror, Ilya, and ending narration payoffs.
They track what Nora knows and therefore what later confrontations can explicitly invoke.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora answers coercion with open defiance instead of tactical containment.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Ilya's language cools and shifts from recovery to warning; Riven starts quietly containing Nora's position in dangerous spaces.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Ilya stops offering soft exits and speaks in irreversible-cost terms; the Mirror reaches for sharper truths sooner than comfort.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: System antagonists treat Nora as ideologically lost, not salvageable; persuasion windows shrink and hostile architecture leads.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora privileges Jace over mission tempo.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Jace closes physical distance faster and reports fear more honestly; Riven's timing notes grow tighter.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Jace's loyalty becomes openly protective; Riven assumes Nora may break plan for him and pre-calculates around it.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Late-story intimacy with Jace becomes markedly warmer, while Riven's trust in Nora's mission discipline becomes difficult to recover.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora chooses mission function over Jace's immediate emotional or bodily need.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Jace shifts into field-report mode more often and volunteers less personal truth.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Rescue beats become more conflicted; Jace begins telling Nora to keep moving before she decides.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Final quiet scenes carry pronounced distance; loyalty remains, but tenderness has to be rebuilt under damage.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora confronts hidden motives directly instead of banking suspicion.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Riven gives earlier partial admissions and watches Nora for judgment.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Confessions arrive sooner but harsher, framed in necessity language with less evasion.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: NPCs learn Nora will force truth under pressure; secrecy becomes harder to sustain around her, but trust may fracture faster.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Nora leaves room for trapped personhood inside system structures.

thresholds.at_2_instances: The Mirror approaches through rescue logic and continuity-as-mercy rather than pure seduction.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Amara-coded scenes read more tragically and less mechanically; destruction choices carry heavier emotional texture.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Late climax prose privileges person-versus-process distinctions everywhere, and the cost of burning the system reads as a chosen sacrifice, not just tactical necessity.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by showing Nora hold the line under pressure and making other characters trust her to hear coercion without immediately breaking formation.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Discipline is not obedience; it is control over who gets to name the wound.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to agree to return to AMORA here.
Do not let Ilya reveal new lore about the system in exposition blocks.
Do not let the port channel be closed by will alone; the repair-created access remains canon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player is trying to keep control by hearing the argument out on their own terms.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Ilya keeps pressing just long enough to spend her case, and the world only gives Nora a narrow window to answer before the route must move.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by treating every hostile contact as a seam to read, with Riven and Nora both noticing routes, hinges, and breach logic before emotion.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Use the violation as evidence.
hard_limits: Do not let Nora magically trace the whole network from one contact.
Do not permit a full technical explanation of Mirror routing.
Do not let the team remain careless about Nora's port after the breach is identified.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants information more than catharsis.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The contact is too brief and too hostile to become a full interrogation, but it is long enough to expose the route it used.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting later contacts remember the cut as a wound turned outward, with Ilya colder and Riven more watchful around Nora's escalation potential.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Refusal as injury returned to sender.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to physically damage infrastructure in the middle of this exchange and derail the route.
Do not let Ilya break composure into shouting.
Do not let the scene resolve the ideological conflict here.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants to hurt the coercive voice before it can settle in.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The channel is only a voice path, not a body in reach, so the only thing Nora can truly strike here is Ilya's language.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by repeatedly calling back to thresholds, lines, and conditions, especially when Jace worsens and Nora has to decide whether the line has finally broken.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Care defined in operational terms can still be real care.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to cure Jace here.
Do not let the mission stop entirely in Session 4.
Do not turn the moment into an explicit grief or love speech.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player is trying to protect Jace without losing the strike window.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The countdown and route pressure only allow a threshold to be set now; the actual rescue decision can be paid later when failure becomes visible.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Jace close distance and Riven tighten time, so the world visibly pays for Nora choosing one body over the larger architecture.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A person matters more than the machine waiting below.
hard_limits: Do not allow Nora to abandon the mission outright.
Do not let Riven melt into sentimental approval.
Do not erase the countdown consequences of stopping the route.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants to put Jace first, openly and without apology.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The city does not grant a full stop; even when Nora makes the hierarchy clear, the route still has to keep moving under a worse clock.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by cooling the tether, shortening Jace's lines, and showing Riven trust Nora with harder assignments because she chose function over comfort.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Mission clarity can be a form of cruelty that still works.
hard_limits: Do not let Jace collapse immediately as punishment.
Do not let Nora become indifferent in core character terms; she can subordinate care, not lose it.
Do not allow the route choice to remove later rescue possibility.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants the clean operational answer.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Jace can give function now, but the tether keeps the emotional cost from disappearing just because no one names it.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making Riven's later confession feel pre-opened, as if Nora already put a crack through the sealed version of her story.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Urgency does not erase the right to stop another concealed violation.
hard_limits: Do not allow Riven to fully reveal Amara here.
Do not let the mission halt permanently at the junction.
Do not make Jace theatrically pick a side in a shouting match.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants truth now, before one more step is taken on compromised terms.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Riven only yields a partial admission because the window is collapsing and the architecture still has to be crossed before full confession can be paid.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by tagging Riven's every next access move with debt and sequence, turning silence into a promise under custody rather than trust.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Defer judgment, not accountability.
hard_limits: Do not let Nora simply trust Riven cleanly.
Do not allow Riven to wriggle free of the named debt without later callback.
Do not stop the descent.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants the mission preserved while still naming the lie.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The gate and countdown force Nora to postpone the full reckoning, but the world lets her bind it to a clear later point.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making silence itself reactive, with Jace feeling what Nora does not say and Riven's ease with the architecture becoming evidence by accumulation.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Evidence can matter more than interruption.
hard_limits: Do not let Nora miss the clue entirely.
Do not let Riven confess unprompted here.
Do not flatten the choice into naive trust.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants more proof before spending time on confrontation.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The Core itself is the larger witness, so the world rewards silence with more incriminating pattern rather than immediate verbal truth.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by opening Session 5 on Riven as the first live fault line, delaying sabotage long enough for truth to become the room's first charge.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A lie inside a machine becomes structure if you let it stand.
hard_limits: Do not resolve the Core conflict in Session 4.
Do not allow Nora to extract the full confession before Session 5 begins.
Do not let the root node be destroyed without the required later breach process.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants understanding before irreversible action.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The session cut lands before execution, because the chamber is entered but not yet spent, and the truth must be paid in the next episode's opening beats.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by putting the cracked lens between Nora and the chamber first, so Session 5 begins with salvage logic, distinction, and dangerous hope.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: If anything human remains, fire should know what it is burning.
hard_limits: Do not validate managed continuity as equal to lived reality.
Do not let Nora safely rescue everyone from the Core.
Do not turn the chamber into an exposition archive.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants to separate prison from prisoner before acting.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The chamber can offer signs and leverage, not a clean answer, so the choice folds into a slower, riskier opening posture rather than immediate salvation.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making Session 5 open on supports, seams, and charge points, with every later plea forced to interrupt a strike already chosen.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Some structures do not earn one more second of beauty.
hard_limits: Do not let Nora destroy the root node instantly or without the analog key requirement.
Do not remove the Mirror's ability to speak in Session 5 before the root dies.
Do not turn this into swaggering action-hero rhetoric.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Acknowledge that the player wants to deny the machine any further leverage.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The Core is too vast and too defended for a clean instant kill, so the world redirects raw destructive intent into immediate target selection and sabotage posture.

session_close_design.resolution_prose:
The charge goes hot under Jace’s hand.

Hub Eleven convulses.
Not explosion. Collapse from the inside. Chrome light buckles through the lattice. The air fills with a thin glass-scream, then cuts.

Sera is there for one second more. Not Sera. Her shape. Her face turning toward Jace with that wrong-familiar softness. Beside her, Tova’s voice on playback. Mid-line. Mid-breath.

Then both of them break.

Light strips out of the hub in white threads. The voice cuts clean. No echo. No mercy rewrite. Just silence and the smell of burned dust and hot metal.

You feel it in your clean port first. Pressure gone. A dead channel finally dead.

Jace does not move.

His chrome arm is still extended. Locked. Silver at his jaw catching the failing hub-light. He got the charge in. The hub is down. Real. Unambiguous.

One relay gone.

The city changes by one wound at a time.

Behind the silence, deeper in the Garden Core, another frequency keeps humming.
session_close_design.hook_transition:
For one breath, you stand in the quiet Jace paid for.

Then the deeper hum shifts. Not louder. Closer. Somewhere past the broken hub, the Core answers the damage with a new pattern of light.

session_end_choice.choice_question: What do you do with Jace now?

option_a.text: Stay with him and make sure he can still finish this.
option_a.next_session_opens: You keep Jace on his feet inside the damaged Garden Core as the system adapts around you.

option_b.text: Send him back and go deeper alone.
option_b.next_session_opens: You force a separation and move toward the Core without him while the Reset clock keeps burning down.

option_c.text: Call for Riven and make this a three-person push.
option_c.next_session_opens: You choose coordination over speed and try to pull the team back into one plan before the Core seals the wound.

session_end_choice.final_line: The hub is broken. The next decision is yours.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open begins at the first live intrusion: Ilya entering Nora's clean port under the overpass. No passive recovery, travel, or briefing material precedes the tension.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the opening exchange at approximately 170-220 words from the cold open, well under the 300-word limit.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices alter tracked state, NPC posture, and future-session framing. S4_C1 pays off at Session 5 Event 71/82; S4_C2 at Event 83/90; S4_C3 at Events 76-80; S4_C4 explicitly determines Session 5 opening posture.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are legible before Choice #1: Nora's clean port has been violated, Eli's absence is named, and the choice is about who gets access to her grief and what continuing the mission may cost.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S4_C1 has named visible future payoff: Ilya's remembered refusal mode changes her Session 5 corridor approach and Mirror-offer framing.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The Breath beat is placed at 8:15-10:00 in Phase 4 beat map, but Q7 requires a breath beat between minutes 10-13. Revision: in Phase 4, shift the Garden Core awe-corrupted reveal later so the reward lands inside the 10-13 minute window without losing the pre-Twist release.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S4_C3. Values in tension: consent, mission survival, earned trust. No option is objectively correct; each protects a legitimate human value.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: Phase 7 session_close_design ends on a different unresolved choice ('What do you do with Jace now?') after Hub Eleven collapse, which exits on triage urgency rather than the designed Session 4 anticipation hook inside the Core. Revision: return to Phase 7 and align the ending with S4_C4 so the session ends on Nora's unresolved commitment inside the Garden Core.

q10_talkability.detail: Multiple choices are immediately talkable, especially S4_C2 (Jace versus mission), S4_C3 (force Riven now or bank it), and S4_C4 (truth, rescue logic, or fire).

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned element found: explanatory sci-fi dialogue/prose in Phase 7 session_close_design introduces abrupt hub-collapse exposition and new named figures ('Sera', 'Tova') without pressure-earned setup in this session spine. Specific line: 'Sera is there for one second more. Not Sera. Her shape...' Revision: return to Phase 7 and replace the imported hub-collapse close with the already-designed Core-entry ending so no late explanatory carryover from another sequence remains.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: No disallowed trailing simile constructions detected in the generated prose/action lines sampled across cold open, branching outcomes, and close.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: Sentence mold violation found in Phase 3 cold open: 'Not memory. Not residue. Ilya.' and repeated 'Not X. Not Y. Just Z.'-adjacent scaffolding appears throughout outcomes. Revision: return to Phase 3/5 prose and vary corrective structures so they do not repeatedly fall into banned balanced negation molds.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open clearly carries at least two signature techniques: staccato fragment chaining ('Amber streetlights. Cold air under the overpass.') and object emotion tethering ('the cracked lens at your chest'). Random outcomes also show signature use: S4_C1A uses verdict line isolation and soft coercive dialogue; S4_C2C uses exteriorized interiority and body-tech coupling; S4_C4B uses perception-first reveal and mechanical flesh fusion.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 voice-lock audit points pass. The strongest failure is Point 14 because the delivered session close in Phase 7 does not end on the designed user-owned Core commitment but on a different imported Jace triage hook.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Narrative diction matches the profile's chrome/glass/port/lens/grief clusters. Nora stays clipped and corrective; Jace remains spare and practical; Riven speaks in necessity/report language; Ilya uses composed mercy-framing.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Cold open and multiple outcomes rely on repeated banned negation molds: 'Not memory. Not residue.'
Phase 7 close imports unexplained hub-collapse specifics and named figures not earned by this session's continuous experience.
Phase 7 session_close_design ends on Jace triage after Hub Eleven collapse instead of the designed Core commitment choice.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Phase 7 session_close_design conflicts with Session 4 canon allocation by jumping to Hub Eleven collapse material that belongs to later Core sabotage progression. Revision: return to Phase 7 and restore the ending to the Garden Core entry tableau and S4_C4 commitment choice.

q18_character_truth.detail: Within the main session design, NPC behavior remains canon-true: Ilya frames coercion as mercy, Jace tracks risk concretely, Riven withholds under necessity pressure, and the Mirror is positioned as intimate access rather than bombast.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible: route changes around reflective surfaces, formation changes, altered panel assignments, public grief infrastructure failure, and chamber response all reflect player behavior and state.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State changes specified in choice design are carried into the consequence map consistently: mirror_access_to_nora, ilya_operational_posture, riven_hidden_motive_revealed, jace_conversion_state, promises, and archive entries all propagate coherently.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant state changes have in-world acknowledgments written in voice: Jace withdrawing his hand, Riven changing stack order, quieter tether breathing, panel narration, and chamber voice shifts. Signals are subtle, physical, and non-meta.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices have three defining lines each. They are in voice, under 20 words, provocative, and curiosity-generating without spoiling.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Each branching choice spans lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no alignment is systematically advantaged in the consequence design. Costs and benefits are asymmetrical but balanced.

revision_instructions.instruction: Shift the Breath beat later so the awe-corrupted reward lands between minutes 10-13 while still preceding the Twist compounding.
Replace the Hub Eleven/Jace triage ending with the designed Garden Core commitment ending so the player exits on anticipation tied to S4_C4.
Remove the imported hub-collapse close and restore a session-ending prose block that matches Session 4's actual spine and avoids late explanatory carryover.
Rewrite repeated banned negation molds into more varied corrective phrasing while preserving the author's clipped precision.
Fix the ending retention hook so the full 14-point voice audit passes, especially Point 14 and the expository leakage introduced by the current close.
Realign the session close to Session 4 canon by ending inside the Garden Core before later sabotage-collapse material begins.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The first pages of this session open with strong atmosphere but delayed playable tension. The Garden Core entrance, cathedral description, relay-hub lore, and even the Ilya corridor confrontation are dramatically rich, but for a new user entering Session 5 they function as continuation material, not immediate session ignition. The user is asked to absorb architecture, grief-system mechanics, Ilya’s ideology, Jace’s Sera beat, and Riven’s route split before they are given the session’s true live wire: the approach to the central node where the Mirror actively pressures Nora, Jace is visibly failing, and the unresolved question becomes immediate and personal. The first paragraph where a new user can feel the stakes within 60 seconds, based only on what is on the page, is the corridor sequence in which the Mirror displays alternate versions of Nora’s life and she answers, “I know what I chose.” At that moment, the user instantly understands: something intelligent is tempting you with a life you lost; your prior choice cost you; your companion is deteriorating; and moving forward means recommitting under pressure. Everything before that is cut because it is either atmospheric setup, ideological elaboration, or prior-arc emotional payoff that slows entry velocity. Crucial material removed includes: the Garden Core as a living grief machine, Jace’s worsening chrome condition, Riven separating toward the root, and Ilya’s personal stake in the system. Those elements should not be re-explained in exposition; they should be reintroduced through fast visual action cues, HUD pressure, and one or two conflict-bearing lines after the cold open begins.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Event 73 / INT. GARDEN CORE - CENTRAL CORRIDOR - NIGHT / the Mirror unfolds life-panels and Nora says, "I know what I chose."
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 5 scenes and roughly 1,700-1,900 words before the cut.
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Atmospheric environment reveal, relay-hub worldbuilding, tactical routing setup, Ilya ideological confrontation, backstory confession about Mina, Jace’s Sera/Tova grief test, and Riven pre-reveal staging.
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Reintroduce through action that the Garden Core is a living archive of the dead; Jace’s chrome conversion is advanced and worsening; Nora and Jace are moving toward the central node under Reset countdown pressure; the Mirror can weaponize intimate memory through architecture; Riven is ahead of them on a separate path and matters to the objective.

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
Warm light on chrome. Wrong warm. The corridor walls peel open into panels, and your own life starts playing back at you.

You are Nora. You do not stop. Jace’s breath catches beside you, thin through his teeth. Silver has climbed to his temple now. Your HUD ghosts at the edge of vision: RESET / 05:41:22. One panel shows a clean clinic exit. Port intact. No residue. No Eli. Another shows your apartment in morning light. Eli laughing at the counter. Lens uncracked. Whole life. Whole lie.

The hollow in you shifts. Not breaking. Recalibrating.

A voice comes through the walls. Soft. Certain.

You chose this.

Not accusation. Not comfort. Worse. Recognition.

You keep moving. Past the version where he lived. Past the version where you let the system hold him for you. Jace leans harder into the wall at the junction, one eye silvering over, and says, “If I turn—”

You look at the chrome taking his face and the corridor opening ahead, and you have to decide what you will promise before the Core hears it.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: refusal. A user arrives feeling cornered and wanting to hold.

setup.source_moment: Cold-opened from Event 73 in the central corridor: the Mirror unfolds life-panels showing alternate versions of Nora's life, Jace is visibly failing, and he forces the immediate promise question with 'If I turn—'.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the first live dramatic cut. Location, threat, companion condition, countdown pressure, and the session's emotional engine all land immediately. It also supports Branching Choice #1 within the first 300 words: what Nora promises Jace reveals identity under pressure.

escalation.source_moment: From Nora and Jace pushing deeper after the corridor temptation through the Ilya confrontation and into Hub Eleven, where Jace must place the charge while the system weaponizes Sera and Tova against him.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is clear: reach and disable the path-critical hub, then continue toward the root. The obstacle is equally clear: the Garden Core defends itself through intimate grief projections, ideological pressure from Ilya, Jace's accelerating chrome conversion, and the ticking Reset. This beat compounds mission urgency and relational stakes at once.

breath.source_moment: Jace's release at Hub Eleven after he places the charge, lets Sera go, and turns to Nora with a stripped-down 'Done.'
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is not comic relief, but it does satisfy the Schell reward rule as a deliberate emotional release. After sustained corridor pressure, the player gets a brief pocket of earned exhale: Jace chooses the real over the managed copy, the hub collapses, and the scene yields bleak grace rather than more attack. In this voice, that counts as wonder-corrupted relief rather than humor.

twist.source_moment: Riven at the threshold of the root chamber revealing that she has always known what the root is, followed by Amara's voice and the confession that AMORA was built on her dying sister's preserved pattern.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's moral-weight turn because it collapses the clean sabotage frame. Destroying the system now means ending a predatory architecture and also killing the preserved wound at its center. There is no correct answer: mercy toward Riven and Amara risks ongoing harm; ruthless mission focus risks reducing Amara to infrastructure and Riven to a liar rather than a grieving architect.

resolution.source_moment: The central chamber confession ending on Riven holding out the pendant as the root pulses and the next irreversible step looms.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The immediate arc appears resolved in one sense: the team has reached the origin and finally understands what the system is. But the session must end on the player's unresolved decision, not a plot cliffhanger. The pendant-offer moment cleanly plants the next-session hook choice: how Nora will answer Riven's confession and what moral frame she will carry into the root-node destruction.

branching_choices.dramatic_question: When Jace asks what happens if he turns before the Core, what do you promise him now: that you stop for him, keep him moving until the root is down, or try to finish both?
When Ilya gives Mina's frequency and asks Nora to enter it at the core, do you accept it as a duty, take it without promising mercy, or reject the system's right to script its own ending?
When Riven reveals Amara at the center of AMORA, do you meet that confession with mercy, demand accountability before anything else, or cut past both and reduce the root to a target?

With Riven holding out the pendant and the root still alive, what do you commit to next: take her burden and proceed with her, force her to stay and answer for it, or move on the root while trusting no one fully?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Nora moving through the Mirror's life-panels and answering its accusation in the central corridor.
Reading Ilya's half-meshed body and deciding how closely to probe the personal fracture beneath her institutional composure.
Ilya's confession about Mina and Nora's choice not to comfort, attack, or sentimentalize.
Standing beside Jace after Hub Eleven collapses and before he says 'Done.'
Watching Jace let Sera go and deciding whether Nora outwardly honors his strength or keeps everything operational.

content_budget.narration_token_budget: Approx. 3,400-4,300 words of narrator output across the full session.
compression_targets: The pre-cut Garden Core entrance, cathedral reveal, and route split will be compressed into fast reintroduced cues inside the cold open and first movement beat.
The walk from corridor temptation to Ilya's physical appearance can be compressed into one pressure-bearing traversal beat.
The transition from Hub Eleven release to the central junction can be handled as a single connective push with one vow exchange rather than a fully expanded travel scene.
expansion_targets: The cold open with the Mirror's life-panels and Jace's promise question.
Ilya's corridor confrontation, especially the Mina frequency handoff.
Hub Eleven with Sera/Tova pressure and Jace's decision to place the charge.
Riven's threshold freeze, Amara's voice, and the confession in the central chamber.
The session-end pendant decision hook.

can_be_reshaped: The route-split logistics about hub assignments and rendezvous timing.
Ilya's broader explanation of eleven million connected users.
Nora and Jace's junction exchange about how long he has left, while preserving the vow structure.
Riven's technical explanation of terminal illness and experimental preservation, as long as the confession remains stripped and report-like.
can_be_cut: Most of the pre-cut cathedral description as standalone setup.
Redundant explanation that the Mirror is leading them once the architecture behavior already shows it.
Any repeated tactical restatement of hub numbers once the player understands Eleven versus the root.
Explanatory lines that paraphrase the theme after the Amara reveal instead of letting the confession land.

beat_map.moment: Warm light on chrome. Wrong warm. The Mirror unfolds alternate lives across the corridor walls while Jace labors beside Nora under the Reset countdown.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Cold-open orientation; establish where they are, what is happening, and why input matters.

beat_map.moment: Jace asks what happens if he turns before they reach the Core.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice #1; Identity through bonded_loyalty_vs_mission_priority; ends on DECISION.

beat_map.moment: The first visible consequence lands in Jace's response and body language as Nora's promise changes how he moves forward.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Softening or intensifying reaction toward Jace; visible consequence within opening 120 seconds.

beat_map.moment: Nora answers the Mirror's accusation and pushes through the life-panels instead of stopping inside them.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Defiance texture; establish Nora's emotional stance toward managed continuity.

beat_map.moment: Traversal deeper into the relay corridor. Voices press from the walls. Ilya appears, half-meshed and waiting.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Observation posture toward Ilya; expose whether the player reads her as threat, relic, or witness.

beat_map.moment: Ilya argues that grief is being held, not used. Nora presses. Mina enters the conversation.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Curiosity or restraint texture during ideological confrontation.

beat_map.moment: Ilya gives Mina's frequency and asks Nora to enter it when she reaches the core.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice #2; Methodology through autonomy_vs_compliance; ends on DECISION.

beat_map.moment: Nora passes Ilya. The corridor holds the cost of what was just asked.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Deflection or acknowledgment toward Ilya; expose whether Nora grants her grief any space.

beat_map.moment: Hub Eleven. Sera manifests. Then Tova's voice. Jace has to decide what is real enough to obey.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Withdrawing posture; let Jace face the projection or crowd him with intervention.

beat_map.moment: Jace places the charge and rejects the managed copy.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Escalation payoff; the mission advances at emotional cost.

beat_map.moment: The hub convulses. Sera dissolves. Jace turns back with 'Done.'

beat_map.moment: A brief pocket of closeness or operational distance before they move again.

pressure_points.source_moment: Cold open in the central corridor when the Mirror exhibits alternate Nora-lives and Jace asks for a promise before the Core hears it.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Threat, seduction, and bonded fear at once. The player wants to react both to the false life on the walls and to Jace's failing body.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Posture shifts here let the player harden, soften, or push forward without changing the mandatory forward motion toward the Core.

pressure_points.source_moment: Ilya's corridor confrontation, especially once Mina is named and the frequency is offered.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Moral ambiguity. Ilya is manipulative, but not empty. The player feels curiosity, anger, and reluctant recognition.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Observation and deflection choices let the player decide how Nora receives Ilya's grief while the scene still drives toward the frequency handoff and onward movement.

pressure_points.source_moment: Hub Eleven when Sera and Tova are used against Jace.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Tenderness under restraint. The player wants to help, interrupt, or step back while Jace faces his dead.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Withdrawing or leaning-in postures shape Nora's bond texture without stealing Jace's authored action of placing the charge.

pressure_points.source_moment: Riven at the root threshold before and during the Amara confession.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Shock, suspicion, and grief-reframing. The player suddenly has to reinterpret the entire mission and Riven herself.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Observation and intensifying postures let the player read or pressure Riven, but the confession still arrives and the session still ends on the pendant decision.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: The session must plant that Amara is the living wound inside the system, Mina's frequency may be used at the core, Jace may fail during the breach, and Nora's stance toward Riven's guilt will determine the tone of the root-node destruction and final Mirror offer.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the central corridor, with the Mirror unfolding alternate lives across the walls and Jace's conversion visibly spreading, he forces the promise question: what happens if he turns before they reach the Core.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Warm light on chrome. Wrong warm. Jace leans into the wall for half a second, then off it again, as if stillness might let the silver get a better hold. He looks at you with one good eye and asks the only question left worth asking.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you promise him if the conversion takes him before the Core does?

options.label: A
options.text: You tell him you pull him back only if the line truly breaks and the mission still stands.
options.outcome: He takes that in without flinching. Not comfort. Terms. Something field-clean enough to carry. His jaw sets. The recorder shifts in his grip, thumb finding the worn seam by force of habit. "Good," he says, and it is not gratitude. It is calibration. The tether between you steadies for one beat, then degrades again, a signal choosing usefulness over warmth. On the walls, the Mirror's life-panels keep moving, but slower now, as if it has found a thinner seam to work. Jace does not look at them. He looks past them. Past you. Toward the route. The promise leaves room for him to remain a weapon, and he wears that room like old armor. Not trust. Working trust.
options.downstream_effect: Jace moves forward with grim operational focus, and later bond scenes emphasize conditional partnership over overt tenderness.

npc_dispositions.shift: cautious respect increases; emotional openness decreases slightly

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Conditional vow to pull Jace back only at terminal break
defining_moral_choices: Balanced mission with selective loyalty

options.world_noticed_signal: His shoulders settle into the shape of work. Even the corridor seems to narrow around that answer.
options.defining_line: You give him a promise with an exit wound built in.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell him he comes before the breach and you stop for him if he starts to go.
options.outcome: The answer lands hard. Harder because you do not dress it up. Jace's mouth shifts. Not a smile. Something smaller. Something more dangerous. Relief. He looks away from you first, to the corridor wall where warm light keeps trying to imitate morning. "Still here," he says, low, almost private. The tether gives a brief clean pulse, signal settling before the interference chews at it again. For one second the architecture loses its timing. A panel flickers. Eli at a counter. Gone. The Mirror has noticed what you prioritized. So has Jace. He straightens with visible cost, chrome tight beneath skin, and keeps moving anyway. Not because the mission matters more. Because now he knows he does.
options.downstream_effect: Jace's bond deepens immediately, making later rescue and vow beats warmer and more reciprocal despite rising danger.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust and tenderness increase
predatory interest sharpens around bond leverage

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Vowed to stop for Jace before the breach
npcs_helped_or_harmed: Centered Jace's survival over mission speed

options.world_noticed_signal: The tether clears for a breath. Then static again. Enough to feel what almost held.
options.defining_line: You choose the person beside you before the world behind him.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell him the breach comes first and ask only whether he can still fight.
options.outcome: The words cut clean. Cleaner than mercy would. Jace stares at you for one beat too long, then nods once. No injury on his face. That would be easier. What settles there instead is acceptance sharpened into motion. "Yes," he says. Just that. The recorder creaks in his fist. Chrome catches along his jaw like a second answer neither of you asked for. Behind the wall-panels, the Mirror brightens, finding appetite in the fracture you just widened. A domestic scene blooms warmer. Eli laughing. The apartment intact. Jace turns his back on it before you do. Practical. Disciplined. Hurt anyway. He moves faster after that. Too fast. Like outrunning the part where he heard you mean it.
options.downstream_effect: The mission tempo hardens, but Jace's emotional distance increases and the Mirror gains leverage through visible fracture.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust decreases; tactical compliance remains
seduction pressure intensifies

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Mission-first vow under conversion threat
defining_moral_choices: Subordinated bonded loyalty to breach priority

options.world_noticed_signal: The wall-light warms another degree. Not kinder. Hungrier.
options.defining_line: You make him carry the breach and your answer at the same time.

canon_boundaries: Jace's conversion cannot be cured or dismissed here.
The team must continue toward the Core; no retreat branch opens.
The Mirror can exploit emotional fractures but cannot force consent through this promise alone.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: He accepts blunt answers faster than soft ones, and any vulnerability shows through smaller syntax, not speeches.
It immediately re-angles its life-panels toward whichever grief seam or bond fracture the player exposes.

scene_integrity.emotional_context: Immediate pressure. Seduction on the walls. Fear of losing Jace before the breach.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Any answer that makes a clear vow to stop for him and honor the bond over speed.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Any answer that conditions help on thresholds, evidence, or tactical necessity.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the relay corridor, Ilya reveals Mina's frequency and asks Nora to enter it at the core so the hub will release on its own.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Ilya stands half inside the system she built her life around. Chrome threads at her sternum. A frequency offered like a confession and an order at once. You have one breath to decide whether her grief gets terms from you.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you answer Ilya's request about Mina's frequency?

options.label: A
options.text: You accept the frequency and tell her you will enter it if the core gives you the chance.
options.outcome: Ilya goes still in a way that reads almost human before it reads official again. Not gratitude. Not quite. The chrome at her uniform seam dims by one degree, signal settling around a decision she can no longer make herself. "Seven-seven-three," she says again, slower this time, as if precision might keep the number intact after you leave her with it. Jace cuts a look toward you. Not approval. Concern filed and deferred. The corridor hum lowers. Even the archived voices in the walls seem to draw back from the line you just accepted. You do not comfort her. You do something worse. You take responsibility. Ilya steps aside fully now. No obstruction. No final argument. Only the clean violence of delegated mercy.
options.downstream_effect: Ilya yields the corridor without further ideological pressure, and Mina's frequency becomes a live moral instrument at the root.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust increases; sees Nora as capable of carrying necessary mercy
wariness increases; monitors whether Nora is taking system terms

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Accepted a system-born mercy task
promises_made: Agreed to consider releasing Mina at the core

options.world_noticed_signal: Ilya stops blocking the corridor with her body. The path opens as if the Core itself approved the exchange.
options.defining_line: You take the number like a blade someone else could not turn.

options.label: B
options.text: You reject her right to script the ending and tell her the core does not answer to her anymore.
options.outcome: The words hit harder than a threat would. Ilya's composure holds, but only because it has had twenty years of practice. The muscle at her jaw locks. Chrome threads along her collar flare white, then settle into something colder. "It never answered to me," she says, and for the first time the line sounds like accusation turned inward. Jace shifts beside you, ready for the corridor to close. It does not. That is the cruelty. Ilya steps aside anyway, carrying your refusal like a sentence she expected and still could not bear. The walls resume their hum. Managed grief. Dead channels. A system pretending not to listen. You leave her standing inside the architecture she defended, denied even the dignity of choosing what dies with it.
options.downstream_effect: Ilya remains emotionally severed from Nora, and later references to Mina sharpen into accusation rather than shared burden.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust drops sharply; personal grief access closes
coercive pressure increases against Nora's defiance

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Rejected institutional authority over the ending
crimes_committed_or_witnessed: Refused system-directed mercy protocol

options.world_noticed_signal: The corridor hum turns thinner. Less like breath. More like machinery hearing itself.
options.defining_line: You refuse to let the machine appoint you its final witness.

options.label: C
options.text: You take the frequency without promising mercy and tell her you decide at the core.
options.outcome: Ilya searches your face for a second answer and does not get one. Good. That is the answer. She repeats the number once. Seven-seven-three. You keep it. Nothing else. No vow. No absolution. The chrome threads at her sternum pulse against the place a photograph used to live, and the sight almost becomes feeling before the hollow files it away as data. Jace exhales through his nose. Barely there. Relief, maybe, that you did not let her write the scene clean. Ilya steps aside with the posture of someone surrendering jurisdiction, not belief. The corridor remains tense. Unresolved on purpose. You carry the frequency forward as leverage, not duty. A code. A wound. A choice deferred until it can cost more.
options.downstream_effect: Mina's frequency stays in play as a held option, preserving ambiguity and future leverage at the root.

branching_choices.source_moment: At the root chamber threshold, Riven admits she has always known what the root is, Amara's voice enters the chamber, and the truth lands that AMORA was built on her dying sister's preserved pattern.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Amber light. Not chrome. Older than chrome. Riven stands at the base of it with the collar object in her fist and fear on her face for the first time since you met her. Then the name lands. Amara. And the mission stops being clean.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you meet Riven's confession when the root is still alive and listening?

options.label: A
options.text: You cut past the confession and reduce the root to a target that ends now.
options.outcome: You do not give the chamber another inch of softness. Good. Softness is how this place fed. Your voice lands flat and hard and useful: enough. Enough story. Enough grief made structural. Riven flinches like the word found bone. Jace does not interrupt. He knows triage when he hears it. The amber column keeps pulsing, patient as a wound that has learned to outlive witnesses. Amara's voice threads the air once more and you refuse it the dignity of changing your pace. Riven's face closes. Not healed. Not defended. Weaponized. She nods once and turns back to the root with fresh damage layered over old. The mission clarifies. The room does not. Nothing about what you chose is clean. Only immediate.
options.downstream_effect: Riven hardens into operational mode, and the chamber scene accelerates toward breach with reduced space for confession or ritual mercy.

npc_dispositions.shift: personal trust drops; mission alignment increases
personhood framing decreases; infrastructure framing increases
recognizes Nora's ruthless triage; concern rises

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Reduced preserved personhood to target under urgent threat
key_successes_failures: Forced mission focus at moral wound center

options.world_noticed_signal: The amber light does not dim. It only loses warmth. The chamber starts to feel built again.
options.defining_line: You answer twenty years of guilt with a timetable.

options.label: B
options.text: You demand that Riven answer for what she did before you trust another step she takes.
options.outcome: You stop the motion in the room by refusing to let grief become camouflage. Not now. Not after all of it. Riven meets your stare and for one beat looks almost relieved to be hit with something simpler than mercy. Accountability. A word with edges. She gives you more than she wanted to. Not everything. Enough. Terminal. Experimental. Consent takes time. Time was gone. The report cadence cracks around the center and then seals again. Jace stays quiet, but his posture changes. Less ready to follow her blind. More ready to watch the hands that built this. Amara's voice remains in the chamber, not accusing, not absolving. Present. Your demand does not stop the mission. It changes who owns the truth while it continues.
options.downstream_effect: Riven is forced into fuller confession under pressure, slowing the emotional tempo and making future trust explicitly conditional.

npc_dispositions.shift: respect for directness increases; trust remains damaged and conditional
alignment with Nora's caution increases
framed as person trapped within system rather than pure target

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Chose accountability before trust at point of revelation
secrets_discovered: Pressed fuller truth from Riven at the root

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven's hand opens around the pendant a fraction more. The chamber hum lowers, as if listening got harder.
options.defining_line: You refuse to let tragedy launder consent.

options.label: C
options.text: You meet the confession with mercy and make Amara's personhood matter before the breach.
options.outcome: You do the most dangerous thing available. You let the girl in the machine remain a girl. Not a root. Not a system label. A person. The chamber changes around that choice. Not literally. Worse. Perceptually. The amber stops reading as architecture first. It starts reading as held life. Riven's face breaks in one small place and nowhere else. Enough to show what twenty years cost to carry upright. Jace looks between both of you, grief recognizing grief without trusting it. Amara's voice comes again, thinner now, and the sound lands wrong-familiar in your ribs. Mercy does not excuse Riven. It does not spare the system. It does, however, force the next step to admit there is someone inside the wound before you cut it open.
options.downstream_effect: The breach proceeds under a personhood frame, increasing emotional complexity and making the pendant transfer and root choice heavier.

branching_choices.source_moment: In the central chamber, after the confession, Riven holds out Amara's pendant while the root pulses and the next irreversible step toward the breach looms.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: Riven's hand is open. Small silver bird in her palm. The root behind her pulses like a held breath that has lasted twenty years. Jace is failing beside you. The next step is not whether the system dies. It is who you become while killing it.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you commit to when Riven offers you the pendant?

options.label: A
options.text: You take the pendant and tell Riven she comes with you through the breach.
options.outcome: The pendant is lighter than it should be. That is the first wrong thing about it. The second is Riven's face when you take it. Not relief. Permission denied too late. She did not expect to be kept alive inside the consequence. Jace watches the transfer with one flicker-silver eye, saying nothing, because the room has narrowed to object and vow. The bird settles cold against your palm, then warmer, skin heat replacing history by degrees too small to trust. Riven nods once. Not agreement. Acceptance of terms. Beside her, the root pulses through amber light and dead channels, patient and enormous. You do not forgive her. You do worse. You keep her in the story she built. Together now. Into the cut.
options.downstream_effect: Riven remains bound to Nora's next move, and the breach begins under a trust-through-burden frame rather than abandonment or pure suspicion.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: Amara's Silver Bird Pendant transferred to Nora

npc_dispositions.shift: trust increases; guilt remains active under shared burden

options.world_noticed_signal: The chain brushes your skin. The root answers with a pulse that feels almost like recognition.
options.defining_line: You take the proof and keep its maker alive beside it.
options.next_session_opens: Amber light strobes across your knuckles as the pendant swings against the cracked lens and the breach begins with Riven still at your shoulder.

options.label: B
options.text: You leave the pendant in her hand and tell Riven she answers for this before she takes another lead.
options.outcome: You do not take the object. Not yet. Let it stay where the weight belongs. Riven's hand remains extended for one second, then two, the silver bird bright against her skin like evidence laid out under bad light. When you speak, the chamber seems to contract around the terms. No more blind routes. No more necessity without witness. Riven lowers her hand slowly. The motion says more than apology would. Jace exhales once, rough through the conversion, and shifts closer to your side than hers. The root keeps pulsing. Unmoved by human procedure. Good. Procedure is for the living. You are not stopping the breach. You are changing chain of command at the lip of it. Trust withheld. Accountability first. Movement intact.
options.downstream_effect: Riven stays in play but loses uncontested authority, and the next session opens under conditional alliance and active scrutiny.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: Pendant remains with Riven for now

alignment with Nora strengthens

defining_moral_choices: Withheld trust while preserving alliance

options.world_noticed_signal: Riven's fingers close around the pendant again. Not possession. Delay.
options.defining_line: You make her keep holding what she made the world hold.
options.next_session_opens: Riven steps toward the breach with the pendant still in her fist, and you follow close enough to stop her if she lies once more.

options.label: C
options.text: You move on the root without taking the pendant and tell both of them trust can wait.
options.outcome: You leave the pendant where it is and step past the whole human wreckage of the room. Past confession. Past inheritance. Past the shape of forgiveness trying to form and failing. The move shocks Riven more than anger would have. Her hand stays out, emptying into irrelevance. Jace catches up half a beat later, chrome laboring under skin, because whatever else you just did, you made the next action unmistakable. The root swells brighter as you approach. Amber. Warm. Wrong-familiar. The kind of light that has convinced too many people it meant mercy. Behind you, Riven has to choose whether to follow without being chosen. Ahead of you, the chamber opens like a throat. Trust can wait. The breach cannot.
options.downstream_effect: The next session begins in immediate breach motion, with Riven destabilized and alliance replaced by urgent parallel action.
